Inbox proposal merge edge cases
description:
src/dogcat/inbox.py manages .dogcats/inbox.jsonl. Proposals have their own lifecycle (open → closed/tombstone) and the merge driver has a separate code path for them (status finality LWW). Existing tests cover the basic LWW semantics — the gap is in cross-file workflows where the merge driver and CLI interact.

Tests to add (tests/test_inbox_merge.py):

1. **Concurrent accept**: side A and side B both run `dcat inbox accept <id>` for the same proposal, each producing a different issue. Merge: verify both issues land (since accept produces a new issue with a fresh id), and the proposal moves to closed exactly once on the merged side.

2. **Accept-vs-reject**: A accepts, B rejects. Status finality says both end at 'closed', but the rejection reason is on B's side and the accept's resulting issue is on A's side. Both should survive — verify.

3. **Tombstone-vs-edit**: A tombstones a proposal (delete), B edits it (e.g. adds a comment). Tombstone is absorbing — the edit is lost. Verify and document this is intentional.

4. **Cross-file integrity**: when issues.jsonl AND inbox.jsonl both have conflicts in the same merge, the driver invocations are independent — verify both files come out valid and the resulting state is consistent (no proposal points at a non-existent accepted-issue).

Use the existing inbox CLI commands (`dcat inbox accept/reject/list`) rather than poking at the JSONL directly.
acceptance: tests/test_inbox_merge.py covers all four scenarios. Cross-file integrity assertion verifies inbox proposals do not reference issues that don't exist on the merged side.

Same-issue field-level conflict policy: explicit tests + documented behavior
description:
src/dogcat/merge_driver.py module docstring already calls out the limitation: same-issue concurrent edits to DIFFERENT fields are resolved as whole-record LWW — i.e. one side's field changes are silently dropped. Today the post-merge concurrent-edit detector in dcat doctor surfaces this case so it is visible.

What we are missing:

1. **Explicit test for the lossy case**: a test that demonstrates the field-level loss (e.g. A edits title, B edits priority, B wins — title change is lost). Right now this is implicit in test_same_issue_edits_resolve and similar — but never the focal assertion.

2. **Test that doctor surfaces it**: extend test_doctor_post_merge_detects_concurrent_edits to assert the specific warning text is shown, including which fields were affected.

3. **Doc the policy**: ensure docs/merge.md or the README section on git workflow has a clear callout — 'concurrent edits to different fields of the same issue keep only the latest writer's version of the entire record. Use `dcat doctor --post-merge` to detect this'. If no merge doc exists, create one.

This is the 'most likely to surprise users' behavior — making it loud is more important than fixing it (per merge_driver.py module docstring, the fix is schema-breaking and out of scope).

Acceptance: a user reading the docs and running doctor should never be silently bitten by this. If they do hit it, doctor names the issue + fields involved.
acceptance: Explicit field-loss test exists. Doctor surfaces field-level conflicts with the affected field names in its output. Documentation explicitly calls out the policy.

acceptance.new: Explicit field-loss test exists. Doctor surfaces field-level conflicts with the affected field names in its output. Documentation explicitly calls out the policy.

Coverage matrix doc: claims vs tests
description:
Final deliverable for the epic: a doc that ties every claim we make about merge/rebase behavior to at least one test (file:line). When this doc is complete and all rows green, we can credibly say 'dogcat works fine with git merges and rebases'.

Where: docs/merge-coverage.md (new file), or extend an existing doc if there is a natural home.

Structure:

| Claim | Source (docstring / README / CHANGELOG) | Test(s) covering it | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Issues: idempotent merge | merge_driver.py module docstring | tests/test_merge_properties.py:test_idempotency | green |
| Proposals: tombstone is absorbing | merge_driver.py module docstring | tests/test_merge_driver.py:test_same_proposal_tombstone_wins_over_closed | green |
| Cherry-pick of an issue-creating commit lands the issue | (this doc — new claim) | tests/test_git_workflows.py:test_cherry_pick_create | green |
| ...etc | | | |

How to build the matrix:
1. Walk merge_driver.py module docstring, README sections on git workflow, any user-facing docs that promise merge behavior. List every distinct claim.
2. For each claim, point at the test(s) covering it. If none exist, mark 'gap' and link the issue tracking it.
3. Add the matrix to CI somehow — ideally a test that fails if a row is unmapped, but at minimum a CONTRIBUTING note that updates to merge_driver.py docstring require a matrix update.

Should be the LAST child closed in this epic, since it depends on the other tasks landing.
acceptance: docs/merge-coverage.md exists with every claim from merge_driver.py module docstring mapped to at least one test. No row is 'gap' without a linked open issue.

acceptance.new: docs/merge-coverage.md exists with every claim from merge_driver.py module docstring mapped to at least one test. No row is 'gap' without a linked open issue.

title.new: Coverage matrix doc: claims vs tests

description.new:
Final deliverable for the epic: a doc that ties every claim we make about merge/rebase behavior to at least one test (file:line). When this doc is complete and all rows green, we can credibly say 'dogcat works fine with git merges and rebases'.

Where: docs/merge-coverage.md (new file), or extend an existing doc if there is a natural home.

Structure:

| Claim | Source (docstring / README / CHANGELOG) | Test(s) covering it | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Issues: idempotent merge | merge_driver.py module docstring | tests/test_merge_properties.py:test_idempotency | green |
| Proposals: tombstone is absorbing | merge_driver.py module docstring | tests/test_merge_driver.py:test_same_proposal_tombstone_wins_over_closed | green |
| Cherry-pick of an issue-creating commit lands the issue | (this doc — new claim) | tests/test_git_workflows.py:test_cherry_pick_create | green |
| ...etc | | | |

How to build the matrix:
1. Walk merge_driver.py module docstring, README sections on git workflow, any user-facing docs that promise merge behavior. List every distinct claim.
2. For each claim, point at the test(s) covering it. If none exist, mark 'gap' and link the issue tracking it.
3. Add the matrix to CI somehow — ideally a test that fails if a row is unmapped, but at minimum a CONTRIBUTING note that updates to merge_driver.py docstring require a matrix update.

Should be the LAST child closed in this epic, since it depends on the other tasks landing.

title: Coverage matrix doc: claims vs tests
Use is_manual_issue() helper everywhere instead of inlined metadata flag check
description:
The is_manual_issue(metadata) helper exists in models.py:28-34 but is bypassed by inlined 'metadata.get("manual") or metadata.get("no_agent")' at 6+ call sites:

- src/dogcat/cli/_formatting.py:194, :559
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:390, :396
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1311, :1599-1600, :1605, :1801, :1812, :1817
- src/dogcat/tui/shared.py
- src/dogcat/tui/detail_panel.py

Framework test: Test 3 (existing abstraction) + Test 1 (true business-rule duplication, two-flag exception). A future addition like metadata.get('ai_only') would have to be hunted across 10+ sites.

Action: replace each inline expression with is_manual_issue(issue.metadata).

title: Use is_manual_issue() helper everywhere instead of inlined metadata flag check
Consolidate hidden --all-namespaces / --namespace shim parameters
description:
The same 13-line hidden parameter declaration block is copied across ~10 commands purely to avoid breaking shell completion / global flag passing. 40 noqa: ARG001 markers across the CLI today.

Sites:
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_close.py:87-98, :170-181, :225-236
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_reopen.py:66-77
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_dep.py:49-60, :153-164
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_comment.py:37-48
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_label.py:41-52
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:636-649, :754-767, :808-819
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1218-1229, :1367-1378, :1888-1899, :1944-1955

Framework test: Test 1 — these change for the same reason (rename a flag, all 10+ copies move together).

Possible approaches:
1. Define HIDDEN_NS_PARAMS as a list of inspect.Parameter objects and splice into command signatures.
2. Wrap with _make_alias-style decorator that injects the params.
3. Move shim handling to the global Typer callback so commands don't need to declare them at all.

Option 3 is the cleanest if Typer allows accepting unknown options globally — investigate first.

Extract shared JSONLAppendStore base for storage and inbox
description:
JSONLStorage._save_locked (storage.py:282-383) and InboxStorage._save (inbox.py:110-138) implement the same atomic-rewrite-via-tempfile pattern: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=self.dogcats_dir, suffix='.jsonl') -> write records -> fsync -> tmp_path.replace(self.path) with identical error wrapping.

Both classes also share:
- _needs_compaction flag
- _lock_path = self.dogcats_dir / '.issues.lock'
- _append() shape (with the trailing-newline check)
- _load() shape (with malformed-last-line tolerance)

Framework test: Test 1 (true duplication) + Test 3 (recurring durability pattern). Recent commits 1368fcb and dcfe702 show this code does change — and any safety improvement (e.g. directory-fsync after rename) must be replicated in both.

Action: Hoist a JSONLAppendStore base class (or at minimum a free-standing _atomic_rewrite_jsonl(path, records, dir) helper). Keep storage-specific concerns (event preservation, tombstone pruning, indexes) in JSONLStorage subclass.

Coordinates with dogcat-2my1 (compaction latency) — refactoring here unblocks performance work.

Split is_json_output into pure is_json + explicit set_json (remove side effect)
description:
_json_state.is_json_output(local_flag=True) currently mutates module-global _global_json as a side effect — calling the getter is also the setter. This is why 6 commands need a magic 'is_json_output(json_output)  # sync local flag for echo_error' line:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_label.py:57
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1383
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_rename_namespace.py:40
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:655
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_config.py:177
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_diff.py:188

Framework test: Test 5 (conditional accumulation / wrong abstraction). The fact that callers need a comment-marked ritual to invoke it is the smell.

Action:
1. Split _json_state into set_json(value: bool) (explicit setter) and is_json() -> bool (pure getter, no args).
2. Each command that has a --json typer option should call set_json(json_output) at the top of its body before any echo_error/typer.echo path.
3. Delete the magic 'sync' lines and their comments.

title: Split is_json_output into pure is_json + explicit set_json (remove side effect)

Extract apply_to_each helper for per-issue-id loop wrappers
description:
7 near-identical wrapper functions exist in CLI commands, each with the same shape: try storage method -> JSON-or-text echo on success -> echo_error(f'<verb> {id}: {e}') and return True on failure. Callers all run 'for id in ids: has_errors = wrapper(...) or has_errors' and 'raise typer.Exit(1)' at end.

Sites:
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_close.py:23-48 (_close_one), :125-149 (_delete_one)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_reopen.py:21-45 (_reopen_one)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_update.py:318-415 (_update_one)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py:345-375 (_close_one), :440-463 (_delete_one), :645-672 (_reject_one)

Framework test: Test 2 (rule of 3 — 7 instances) + Test 1 (change for the same reason: JSON output formatting consistency, error-message templating).

Action: Add to _helpers.py:

  def apply_to_each(
      ids: list[str],
      op: Callable[[str], None],
      verb: str,
      *,
      json_output: bool,
  ) -> int:  # returns exit code
      ...

Each command's wrapper collapses to a single 'op' closure that calls the storage method and echoes success.

title: Extract apply_to_each helper for per-issue-id loop wrappers

Hoist resolve_id to shared resolve_partial_id helper
description:
JSONLStorage.resolve_id (storage.py:592-631) and InboxStorage.resolve_id (inbox.py:256-293) implement the same algorithm: exact match -> suffix match -> ambiguity error with same message format.

Subtle divergence (test for drift after refactor): storage uses split('-', 1)[-1] while inbox uses rsplit('-', 1)[-1]. The inbox can have multi-segment full IDs like 'dogcat-inbox-X' so rsplit is the correct choice; storage's split is a latent bug for any namespace containing a hyphen.

Framework test: Test 1 (same algorithm) + the existing divergence is itself evidence the duplication has drifted.

Action:
1. Create dogcat/_id_resolve.py with: resolve_partial_id(partial: str, ids: Iterable[str]) -> str | None
2. Use rsplit (the correct version) and add a test for hyphenated namespaces.
3. Replace both call sites with a thin wrapper.

Hoist _tracked_changes and _field_value into shared diff helper
description:
Three places define the same enum-normalize-and-diff pattern:

- src/dogcat/storage.py:490-511 (_field_value, _tracked_changes)
- src/dogcat/inbox.py:200-216 (_tracked_changes)
- src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:436-440 (_field_value)

Framework test: Test 1 — these change together when the enum normalization rule changes (or a new value-like type appears).

Action: Move to a new shared module dogcat/_diff.py:

  def field_value(value: Any) -> Any:
      return value.value if hasattr(value, 'value') else value

  def tracked_changes(
      old: dict[str, Any],
      new: dict[str, Any],
      tracked: frozenset[str],
  ) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
      ...

Both classes call into the shared function with their own TRACKED_FIELDS / TRACKED_PROPOSAL_FIELDS constant.

Push _emit_event into _BaseEventLog
description:
JSONLStorage._emit_event (storage.py:460-488) and InboxStorage._emit_event (inbox.py:170-198) both build an EventRecord and best-effort-append to the event log with a debug log on failure. Same shape.

_BaseEventLog already exists at event_log.py:82-146 and abstracts the file/lock layer. The emit-with-best-effort semantics belong there too.

Framework test: Test 1 + Test 3 (base class exists, just doesn't yet capture this pattern).

Action: Add _BaseEventLog.emit(event_type, full_id, updated_at_iso, by, title, changes) that constructs the EventRecord and wraps the append in the best-effort try/except logging block. Call sites in storage.py and inbox.py become one-liners.

Use apply_common_filters in holdout commands (recently_added, pr, list)
description:
_helpers.apply_common_filters (lines 287-353) already centralizes manual/agent/has-comments/namespace filters, but three commands reimplement the same filter loops inline:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1595-1606 (recently_added) — manual/agent_only + has_comments/without_comments
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1798-1824 (pr / progress_review) — same filters duplicated for ip_issues and ir_issues lists (4 copies of the manual predicate)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:386-402 (list) — same filters

Framework test: Test 3 — abstraction exists; bypassing it is how is_manual_issue inlining (sub-issue dogcat-30a6) keeps recurring.

Action: switch these call sites to apply_common_filters(...) with the right keyword args. The 'pr' command's two-list shape may need apply_common_filters called twice (once per status list) — that's fine.

title: Use apply_common_filters in holdout commands (recently_added, pr, list)

Promote _resolve_dogcats_path and reuse instead of inline find_dogcats_dir walks
description:
The idiom 'if not Path(dogcats_dir).is_dir(): dogcats_dir = find_dogcats_dir()' is repeated 6 times in src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py at lines 32-35, 42-50, 217-219, 244-246, 566-568, 703-706.

Meanwhile config.py:229-266 already defines _resolve_dogcats_path doing essentially the same walk-up (with extra .dogcatrc handling).

Framework test: Test 3 — abstraction exists; it's just hidden behind a leading underscore in config.py.

Action:
1. Decide the canonical name (resolve_dogcats_dir) and home (likely _helpers.py or a new _paths.py).
2. Promote/move the function (drop the leading underscore on the public surface).
3. Replace all 6 inline walks in _cmd_inbox.py.

Extract shared local+remote inbox loading helper
description:
The 'lookup inbox_remote -> resolve to .dogcats -> InboxStorage(remote) -> filter by namespace' block (~15 lines) is copied across three commands:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:587-627 (_show_inbox_proposals — handles local + remote)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py:238-290 (inbox list — handles local + remote with grouping)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:190-204 (_show_inbox_in_ready — local only, but shares the local-load shape)

Framework test: Test 1 + Test 2 — three instances; remote-inbox semantics changes (e.g. error handling, namespace filter precedence) need to ripple.

Action: extract to _helpers.py:

  def load_inbox(
      dogcats_dir: str,
      namespace: str | None,
      *,
      all_namespaces: bool,
      show_all: bool = False,
  ) -> tuple[list[Proposal], list[Proposal], str | None]:
      # returns (local, remote, remote_path)

Each call site becomes one line + a formatting block that's already command-specific.

Note: _format_proposal_brief lives in both inbox CLI (line 73-76) and _formatting.py:92-101 (format_proposal_brief). Same opportunity to consolidate, but in a separate change to keep this PR focused.

Drop redundant check_*_exclusive calls (validation runs twice)
description:
_helpers.apply_common_filters validates agent/manual + has/without-comments exclusivity internally (lines 313-316). But several callers also validate the same pair before calling it:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:298-305 (list command)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1462 (recently_closed)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1577 (recently_added)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1786-1787 (pr / progress_review)

The validation runs twice in those paths.

Framework test: Test 5 (conditional accumulation) — validators leaked into a transformation function, so callers re-validate defensively.

Action: pick one — either drop the calls from apply_common_filters (since validation isn't its job) or drop them from the callers. Recommendation: keep them in apply_common_filters (it's the common path) and remove the caller-side checks. Callers that don't use apply_common_filters (e.g. recently-closed) keep their explicit checks.

Apply DRY review findings: consolidate duplication and simplify wrong abstractions
description:
Umbrella epic for follow-ups from the should-i-abstract review (2026-04-25).

Three categories of findings:
- Abstract This: 10 instances of true knowledge duplication where the same algorithm or invariant lives in multiple places.
- Inline This: 3 wrong abstractions where the cost of indirection has grown past the cost of duplication.
- Leave Alone: 7 instances of incidental duplication that should stay separate (documented for future readers, no action).

Sub-issues are scoped to small, independently-shippable units. Pick them up in priority order (P1 first), or batch related ones (e.g. items 5/6/7 all touch the storage/inbox parallelism).

title: Apply DRY review findings: consolidate duplication and simplify wrong abstractions
Use is_manual_issue() helper everywhere instead of inlined metadata flag check
description:
The is_manual_issue(metadata) helper exists in models.py:28-34 but is bypassed by inlined 'metadata.get("manual") or metadata.get("no_agent")' at 6+ call sites:

- src/dogcat/cli/_formatting.py:194, :559
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:390, :396
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1311, :1599-1600, :1605, :1801, :1812, :1817
- src/dogcat/tui/shared.py
- src/dogcat/tui/detail_panel.py

Framework test: Test 3 (existing abstraction) + Test 1 (true business-rule duplication, two-flag exception). A future addition like metadata.get('ai_only') would have to be hunted across 10+ sites.

Action: replace each inline expression with is_manual_issue(issue.metadata).

title: Use is_manual_issue() helper everywhere instead of inlined metadata flag check
Consolidate hidden --all-namespaces / --namespace shim parameters
description:
The same 13-line hidden parameter declaration block is copied across ~10 commands purely to avoid breaking shell completion / global flag passing. 40 noqa: ARG001 markers across the CLI today.

Sites:
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_close.py:87-98, :170-181, :225-236
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_reopen.py:66-77
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_dep.py:49-60, :153-164
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_comment.py:37-48
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_label.py:41-52
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:636-649, :754-767, :808-819
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1218-1229, :1367-1378, :1888-1899, :1944-1955

Framework test: Test 1 — these change for the same reason (rename a flag, all 10+ copies move together).

Possible approaches:
1. Define HIDDEN_NS_PARAMS as a list of inspect.Parameter objects and splice into command signatures.
2. Wrap with _make_alias-style decorator that injects the params.
3. Move shim handling to the global Typer callback so commands don't need to declare them at all.

Option 3 is the cleanest if Typer allows accepting unknown options globally — investigate first.

title: Consolidate hidden --all-namespaces / --namespace shim parameters
Extract shared JSONLAppendStore base for storage and inbox
description:
JSONLStorage._save_locked (storage.py:282-383) and InboxStorage._save (inbox.py:110-138) implement the same atomic-rewrite-via-tempfile pattern: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=self.dogcats_dir, suffix='.jsonl') -> write records -> fsync -> tmp_path.replace(self.path) with identical error wrapping.

Both classes also share:
- _needs_compaction flag
- _lock_path = self.dogcats_dir / '.issues.lock'
- _append() shape (with the trailing-newline check)
- _load() shape (with malformed-last-line tolerance)

Framework test: Test 1 (true duplication) + Test 3 (recurring durability pattern). Recent commits 1368fcb and dcfe702 show this code does change — and any safety improvement (e.g. directory-fsync after rename) must be replicated in both.

Action: Hoist a JSONLAppendStore base class (or at minimum a free-standing _atomic_rewrite_jsonl(path, records, dir) helper). Keep storage-specific concerns (event preservation, tombstone pruning, indexes) in JSONLStorage subclass.

Coordinates with dogcat-2my1 (compaction latency) — refactoring here unblocks performance work.

title: Extract shared JSONLAppendStore base for storage and inbox
Split is_json_output into pure is_json + explicit set_json (remove side effect)
description:
_json_state.is_json_output(local_flag=True) currently mutates module-global _global_json as a side effect — calling the getter is also the setter. This is why 6 commands need a magic 'is_json_output(json_output)  # sync local flag for echo_error' line:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_label.py:57
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1383
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_rename_namespace.py:40
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:655
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_config.py:177
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_diff.py:188

Framework test: Test 5 (conditional accumulation / wrong abstraction). The fact that callers need a comment-marked ritual to invoke it is the smell.

Action:
1. Split _json_state into set_json(value: bool) (explicit setter) and is_json() -> bool (pure getter, no args).
2. Each command that has a --json typer option should call set_json(json_output) at the top of its body before any echo_error/typer.echo path.
3. Delete the magic 'sync' lines and their comments.

title: Split is_json_output into pure is_json + explicit set_json (remove side effect)
Extract apply_to_each helper for per-issue-id loop wrappers
description:
7 near-identical wrapper functions exist in CLI commands, each with the same shape: try storage method -> JSON-or-text echo on success -> echo_error(f'<verb> {id}: {e}') and return True on failure. Callers all run 'for id in ids: has_errors = wrapper(...) or has_errors' and 'raise typer.Exit(1)' at end.

Sites:
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_close.py:23-48 (_close_one), :125-149 (_delete_one)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_reopen.py:21-45 (_reopen_one)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_update.py:318-415 (_update_one)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py:345-375 (_close_one), :440-463 (_delete_one), :645-672 (_reject_one)

Framework test: Test 2 (rule of 3 — 7 instances) + Test 1 (change for the same reason: JSON output formatting consistency, error-message templating).

Action: Add to _helpers.py:

  def apply_to_each(
      ids: list[str],
      op: Callable[[str], None],
      verb: str,
      *,
      json_output: bool,
  ) -> int:  # returns exit code
      ...

Each command's wrapper collapses to a single 'op' closure that calls the storage method and echoes success.

title: Extract apply_to_each helper for per-issue-id loop wrappers
Hoist resolve_id to shared resolve_partial_id helper
description:
JSONLStorage.resolve_id (storage.py:592-631) and InboxStorage.resolve_id (inbox.py:256-293) implement the same algorithm: exact match -> suffix match -> ambiguity error with same message format.

Subtle divergence (test for drift after refactor): storage uses split('-', 1)[-1] while inbox uses rsplit('-', 1)[-1]. The inbox can have multi-segment full IDs like 'dogcat-inbox-X' so rsplit is the correct choice; storage's split is a latent bug for any namespace containing a hyphen.

Framework test: Test 1 (same algorithm) + the existing divergence is itself evidence the duplication has drifted.

Action:
1. Create dogcat/_id_resolve.py with: resolve_partial_id(partial: str, ids: Iterable[str]) -> str | None
2. Use rsplit (the correct version) and add a test for hyphenated namespaces.
3. Replace both call sites with a thin wrapper.

title: Hoist resolve_id to shared resolve_partial_id helper
Hoist _tracked_changes and _field_value into shared diff helper
description:
Three places define the same enum-normalize-and-diff pattern:

- src/dogcat/storage.py:490-511 (_field_value, _tracked_changes)
- src/dogcat/inbox.py:200-216 (_tracked_changes)
- src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:436-440 (_field_value)

Framework test: Test 1 — these change together when the enum normalization rule changes (or a new value-like type appears).

Action: Move to a new shared module dogcat/_diff.py:

  def field_value(value: Any) -> Any:
      return value.value if hasattr(value, 'value') else value

  def tracked_changes(
      old: dict[str, Any],
      new: dict[str, Any],
      tracked: frozenset[str],
  ) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
      ...

Both classes call into the shared function with their own TRACKED_FIELDS / TRACKED_PROPOSAL_FIELDS constant.

title: Hoist _tracked_changes and _field_value into shared diff helper
Push _emit_event into _BaseEventLog
description:
JSONLStorage._emit_event (storage.py:460-488) and InboxStorage._emit_event (inbox.py:170-198) both build an EventRecord and best-effort-append to the event log with a debug log on failure. Same shape.

_BaseEventLog already exists at event_log.py:82-146 and abstracts the file/lock layer. The emit-with-best-effort semantics belong there too.

Framework test: Test 1 + Test 3 (base class exists, just doesn't yet capture this pattern).

Action: Add _BaseEventLog.emit(event_type, full_id, updated_at_iso, by, title, changes) that constructs the EventRecord and wraps the append in the best-effort try/except logging block. Call sites in storage.py and inbox.py become one-liners.

title: Push _emit_event into _BaseEventLog
Use apply_common_filters in holdout commands (recently_added, pr, list)
description:
_helpers.apply_common_filters (lines 287-353) already centralizes manual/agent/has-comments/namespace filters, but three commands reimplement the same filter loops inline:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1595-1606 (recently_added) — manual/agent_only + has_comments/without_comments
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1798-1824 (pr / progress_review) — same filters duplicated for ip_issues and ir_issues lists (4 copies of the manual predicate)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:386-402 (list) — same filters

Framework test: Test 3 — abstraction exists; bypassing it is how is_manual_issue inlining (sub-issue dogcat-30a6) keeps recurring.

Action: switch these call sites to apply_common_filters(...) with the right keyword args. The 'pr' command's two-list shape may need apply_common_filters called twice (once per status list) — that's fine.

title: Use apply_common_filters in holdout commands (recently_added, pr, list)
Promote _resolve_dogcats_path and reuse instead of inline find_dogcats_dir walks
description:
The idiom 'if not Path(dogcats_dir).is_dir(): dogcats_dir = find_dogcats_dir()' is repeated 6 times in src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py at lines 32-35, 42-50, 217-219, 244-246, 566-568, 703-706.

Meanwhile config.py:229-266 already defines _resolve_dogcats_path doing essentially the same walk-up (with extra .dogcatrc handling).

Framework test: Test 3 — abstraction exists; it's just hidden behind a leading underscore in config.py.

Action:
1. Decide the canonical name (resolve_dogcats_dir) and home (likely _helpers.py or a new _paths.py).
2. Promote/move the function (drop the leading underscore on the public surface).
3. Replace all 6 inline walks in _cmd_inbox.py.

title: Promote _resolve_dogcats_path and reuse instead of inline find_dogcats_dir walks
Extract shared local+remote inbox loading helper
description:
The 'lookup inbox_remote -> resolve to .dogcats -> InboxStorage(remote) -> filter by namespace' block (~15 lines) is copied across three commands:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:587-627 (_show_inbox_proposals — handles local + remote)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py:238-290 (inbox list — handles local + remote with grouping)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:190-204 (_show_inbox_in_ready — local only, but shares the local-load shape)

Framework test: Test 1 + Test 2 — three instances; remote-inbox semantics changes (e.g. error handling, namespace filter precedence) need to ripple.

Action: extract to _helpers.py:

      dogcats_dir: str,
      namespace: str | None,
      *,

      show_all: bool = False,

      # returns (local, remote, remote_path)

Each call site becomes one line + a formatting block that's already command-specific.

Note: _format_proposal_brief lives in both inbox CLI (line 73-76) and _formatting.py:92-101 (format_proposal_brief). Same opportunity to consolidate, but in a separate change to keep this PR focused.

title: Extract shared local+remote inbox loading helper
Drop redundant check_*_exclusive calls (validation runs twice)
description:
_helpers.apply_common_filters validates agent/manual + has/without-comments exclusivity internally (lines 313-316). But several callers also validate the same pair before calling it:

- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_read.py:298-305 (list command)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1462 (recently_closed)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1577 (recently_added)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1786-1787 (pr / progress_review)

The validation runs twice in those paths.

Framework test: Test 5 (conditional accumulation) — validators leaked into a transformation function, so callers re-validate defensively.

Action: pick one — either drop the calls from apply_common_filters (since validation isn't its job) or drop them from the callers. Recommendation: keep them in apply_common_filters (it's the common path) and remove the caller-side checks. Callers that don't use apply_common_filters (e.g. recently-closed) keep their explicit checks.

title: Drop redundant check_*_exclusive calls (validation runs twice)
Apply DRY review findings: consolidate duplication and simplify wrong abstractions
description:
Umbrella epic for follow-ups from the should-i-abstract review (2026-04-25).

Three categories of findings:
- Abstract This: 10 instances of true knowledge duplication where the same algorithm or invariant lives in multiple places.
- Inline This: 3 wrong abstractions where the cost of indirection has grown past the cost of duplication.
- Leave Alone: 7 instances of incidental duplication that should stay separate (documented for future readers, no action).

Sub-issues are scoped to small, independently-shippable units. Pick them up in priority order (P1 first), or batch related ones (e.g. items 5/6/7 all touch the storage/inbox parallelism).

title: Apply DRY review findings: consolidate duplication and simplify wrong abstractions
TUI dashboard: issue list clips bottom rows without showing a scrollbar
description:
## Symptom

When the TUI dashboard (`dcat tui`) shows more issues than fit on screen, the bottom rows are silently clipped. There is no scrollbar, so the user has no indication that hidden issues exist and no way to scroll to them.

Reproduced on a terminal where the issue list has ~30 entries; the last few entries (e.g. `dogcat-4xu9`, `dogcat-5h53`, ...) are not visible and there is no vertical scrollbar on the OptionList.

## Root cause

In `src/dogcat/tui/dashboard.py` (CSS for `#issue-list`), the `OptionList` widget uses Textual's default sizing: `height: auto; max-height: 100%`. The `100%` resolves against the full parent height, not the space remaining after the Input above it. So the OptionList wants the full parent height, the Input pushes it down, and the parent Vertical (`overflow: hidden hidden`) clips the overflow.

## Fix

Set an explicit `height: 1fr` on `#issue-list` so it takes the remaining vertical space after the search Input. Textual's OptionList extends `ScrollView`, which has `overflow-y: auto`, so once the height is constrained the vertical scrollbar will appear automatically when content overflows.

## Acceptance

- With many issues open, the dashboard shows a vertical scrollbar on the issue list.
- Arrow / page-down keys scroll the list to reveal previously-hidden issues.
- No visual regression in narrow mode (single-pane) or split-pane mode.

src/dogcat/storage.py:341-344

At minimum log a warning. Ideally refuse compaction when source has unparseable lines so user can recover from git.

Lens: error-gaps (Critical)

InboxStorage silently tolerates malformed mid-file lines
description:
JSONLStorage._load only tolerates a malformed *last* line (storage.py:197-208). InboxStorage._load skips any malformed line silently and sets _needs_compaction (inbox.py:96-103). Mid-file proposal corruption vanishes silently on next compaction.

Pick one policy. The strict one (storage.py) is safer for an audit log. If keeping inbox lenient, document why and surface _needs_compaction via dcat doctor before compaction silently rewrites.

Lens: error-gaps + test-gaps (High)

inbox accept cross-storage flow has no rollback
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py:530-538: storage.create(issue) succeeds, then remote_inbox.close(...) runs separately. If close fails (network, lock, OSError), the local issue exists but the remote proposal is never closed and gets no resolved_issue link — orphaned data.

Extract to services/inbox_promote.py:promote_to_issue(...) and add partial-failure test (monkey-patch close to raise; assert error surfaces and detect orphaned local issue).

Lens: extract-logic + test-gaps (High)

Extract to services/inbox_promote.py:promote_to_issue(...) and add partial-failure test (monkey-patch close to raise; assert error surfaces and detect orphaned local issue).

Lens: extract-logic + test-gaps (High)

typing_extensions is undeclared runtime dependency
description:
src/dogcat/models.py:9 imports `from typing_extensions import Self`. Not in pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]. Currently works only because textual transitively pulls it in. If textual ever drops the dep, dcat breaks at import time.

Fix: add `typing_extensions>=4.0.0` to dependencies. Optionally use stdlib `typing.Self` for Python>=3.11 with a fallback.

Lens: dep-hygiene (High)

Either implement a real check (scan JSONL for duplicate hashes — duplicates can sneak in via concurrent merges or hand-edits) or delete and remove the doctor call.

_is_default_branch treats permission errors as 'safe to compact'
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:399-416: Any non-zero git returncode (permission denied, lock contention, internal error) falls through to `return True` which means "compact freely". The intent is "only compact on default branch to avoid noisy feature-branch diffs". Permission errors silently disable the protection.

Distinguish FileNotFoundError (genuinely no git) from non-zero returncode (git ran, said something). For non-zero, return False (do NOT compact) and log stderr.

Add test that monkey-patches subprocess.run to return non-zero with stderr.

Lens: error-gaps (Medium)

title.new: _is_default_branch treats permission errors as 'safe to compact'

Route workflow list-style commands through existing _list_by_status helper
description:
Seven public list-style commands open-code the same pipeline (~50-line bodies + ~85 lines of identical Typer.Option declarations each = ~800-950 duplicated lines).

src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py: ready (43-191), in_progress (367-496), open_issues (498-627), in_review (629-758), deferred (1082-1211), manual_list (1239-1350), snoozed_list (1919-2061).

Helper _list_by_status already exists at lines 796-868 but is only used by hidden ir/ip aliases. Migrate the public commands to delegate to it. Also: replace recently_closed_alias and recently_added_alias (1611-1658, 1672-1719) with _make_alias calls (mirrors o/b/d aliases at 1660-1670).

Single biggest duplication in the codebase. Lens: duplicates + extract-logic + simplify-code + complexity (4-reviewer consensus).

Merge issue+event records into single locked append (write amplification)
description:
Each storage mutation does TWO file-lock acquisitions and TWO fsyncs to the same file: one for the issue record, one for the event record (via EventLog.emit → EventLog.append which takes its own lock).

Sites: src/dogcat/storage.py:529 (create), 733 (update), 977 (close), 1034 (reopen), 1110 (delete) — each calls _append([record]) followed by _emit_event which goes to event_log.py:101-107.

For a CLI batch like dcat close A B C ... Z (via apply_to_each in _cmd_close.py:80), 26 ids ≈ 52 lock acquisitions and 52 fsyncs.

Fix: have _emit_event accept an open lock context, OR batch issue + event records into one _append([issue_record, event_record]) call covered by one lock and one fsync. Mutation methods already construct both back-to-back.

Lens: query-smells (High).

For a CLI batch like dcat close A B C ... Z (via apply_to_each in _cmd_close.py:80), 26 ids ≈ 52 lock acquisitions and 52 fsyncs.

Fix: have _emit_event accept an open lock context, OR batch issue + event records into one _append([issue_record, event_record]) call covered by one lock and one fsync. Mutation methods already construct both back-to-back.

Lens: query-smells (High).

Web app should hold persistent InboxStorage instead of constructing per request
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:79: _create_proposal_sync builds a brand-new InboxStorage(dogcats_dir=...) on every POST. Constructor calls _load() which readlines() the entire inbox file and parses every record.

Per-submit latency grows linearly with inbox size. Concurrent contention with writers.

Fix: hold one InboxStorage on app.state at app boot. Guard with inbox.reload() only when file mtime/size changed (cheap stat check). The lock already serializes writes; reload-on-change keeps memory state fresh.

Same fix applies to namespace enumeration (app.state.namespaces is currently snapshotted at boot and never refreshed — new namespaces minted via CLI invisible until restart).

Lens: query-smells (High).

Add storage.batch_* methods to amortize lock churn in apply_to_each
description:
cli/_helpers.py:142 (apply_to_each) is used by close, update, reopen, inbox close/delete/reject. Each iteration takes the file lock + fsync independently, AND if compaction-eligible, runs git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD via subprocess — N items = O(N) locks + fsyncs + subprocess forks.

Add storage.batch_close, batch_update, batch_delete that take the lock once and write all records in one append. Cache _is_default_branch result for the duration of one CLI invocation (branch unlikely to change mid-batch).

Lens: query-smells.

Convert validation errors from list[dict[str,str]] to ValidationError dataclass
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:39-388 — 12+ functions return list[dict[str, str]] with implicit {level, message} shape. Consumed by cli/_cmd_doctor.py:66, 247-271, 471-487 which does e['level'] == 'error' string comparisons.

Replace with:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ValidationError:
    level: Literal['error', 'warning']
    message: str

Bump signatures: list[dict[str, str]] → list[ValidationError]. Doctor JSON output stays identical via dataclasses.asdict.

Pyright catches typo'd e['levle']; Literal rules out third-state bugs.

Lens: type-structs.

Refactor merge_jsonl: extract per-strategy helpers
description:
src/dogcat/merge_driver.py:341-460 — merge_jsonl orchestrates 5 distinct merge strategies in one ~120-line function: issues LWW, proposals status-rank LWW, events dedupe, deps three-way, links three-way. Steps 4 and 5 are structurally identical.

Already has _replay_deps/_replay_links helpers. Now extract:
- _merge_issues_lww(ours, theirs)
- _merge_proposals(ours, theirs)
- _merge_events(ours, theirs)
- generic _merge_three_way(base, ours, theirs, key_fn) — used by both deps and links

merge_jsonl becomes ~25 lines of orchestration + assembly.

Add tests for doctor --fix paths (dangling deps, issue_prefix migration)
description:
Two doctor --fix paths are unit-tested in their helpers but not in the doctor wiring:

1. Dangling deps fix at src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:361-373 — calls storage.remove_dependencies. tests/test_cmd_doctor.py has zero coverage (grep for 'dangling_deps' / 'remove_dependencies' returns nothing).
   Suggested test: build JSONL with a dependency referencing a tombstoned issue's full_id, run doctor --fix, assert dep is gone and file is rewritten.

2. issue_prefix → namespace migration at src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:178-192 — calls migrate_config_keys. tests/test_config.py:475+ tests the helper, but the doctor --fix wiring isn't covered.
   Suggested test: write config.toml with deprecated key, run doctor --fix, assert namespace is set and issue_prefix is gone.

Lens: test-gaps (High).

Add stream watchdog Observer integration tests
description:
src/dogcat/stream.py:170-205, 405-414, 538-565 — StreamEmitter.on_modified, on_moved, InboxStreamEmitter.on_modified, and StreamWatcher.start/stop are never directly exercised. tests/test_stream.py calls _handle_file_change() directly, bypassing the watchdog dispatch.

The path-filter logic (endswith('issues.jsonl') vs endswith('inbox.jsonl')) is unverified — a typo like endswith('issue.jsonl') would not be caught.

Suggested:
- Synthesize FileModifiedEvent / FileMovedEvent instances with various src_path/dest_path values; assert the right handler runs (or doesn't).
- One integration test that starts/stops the Observer against a real temp file.

Lens: test-gaps (High).

Doctor writes settings.json with non-atomic write_text
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:660-664, 685-687: _install_precompact_hook and _upgrade_precompact_hook use stdlib json.dumps + settings_path.write_text(...) — only place in the codebase using direct write_text on a settings file. No atomic rename, no file lock.

Two concurrent dcat doctor --fix runs (or doctor + Claude Code modifying the same file) could clobber each other or produce a partial file on a crash. Inconsistent with project's own discipline (atomic_rewrite_jsonl pattern used everywhere else).

Fix: reuse atomic_rewrite_jsonl-style write (write to temp, fsync, replace). Even a shallow helper would suffice. While at it, replace stdlib json with orjson(option=OPT_INDENT_2|OPT_APPEND_NEWLINE) for consistency.

Lens: error-gaps.

Log warning on config.toml parse failure (currently silent)
description:
src/dogcat/config.py:89-97 — _load_toml catches (TOMLDecodeError, OSError) and returns {}. A typo in config.toml produces 'all defaults' with no warning. The user spends time wondering why their namespace='foo' setting doesn't apply.

dcat doctor's config_toml check verifies existence only, not parseability — so the file passes the doctor check while still being broken.

Fix:
1. logger.warning on TOMLDecodeError so LOGLEVEL=WARNING surfaces it.
2. dcat doctor runs _load_toml separately and reports parse errors as a distinct check.

Lens: error-gaps.

Web POST mutates app.state.namespaces before write succeeds
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:241: valid_namespaces.append(namespace) is called BEFORE the asyncio.to_thread(_create_proposal_sync, ...) write. If the write fails, the namespace is still in valid_namespaces for the rest of the process lifetime. Subsequent submissions to that namespace bypass the 'is_new_namespace' branch (they appear to exist) and won't be persisted to pinned_namespaces.

Fix: append only after successful _create_proposal_sync (move next to the existing _persist_pinned_namespace call).

Lens: error-gaps.

Promote _DEFAULT_BRANCHES to constants and respect git's init.defaultBranch
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:397: _DEFAULT_BRANCHES = frozenset({'main', 'master'}) is a class-attribute set used to gate auto-compaction. A project using develop, trunk, or a custom default branch loses auto-compaction silently.

Modern git installs respect init.defaultBranch which can be queried with git config --get init.defaultBranch.

Fix:
1. Move to constants.py as DEFAULT_BRANCH_NAMES
2. _is_default_branch should also read init.defaultBranch from git config and union with the hardcoded fallback

Note: related to dcat-49nq (allow compaction on non-default branches) but distinct — that one is about removing the gate; this is about making the gate config-aware.

Lens: hardcoded.

Move web validation limits + namespace pattern to constants.py (share with CLI)
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:25-32 has MAX_TITLE_LEN=500, MAX_DESC_LEN=50000, MAX_NAMESPACE_LEN=64, NAMESPACE_PATTERN regex. These are the ONLY namespace shape rules in the codebase, but namespaces flow through idgen.py, storage.py, inbox.py, config.py (pinned_namespaces), and CLI (--namespace).

CLI accepts any string today; web rejects via regex. Inconsistent enforcement.

Fix:
1. Promote to constants.py with is_valid_namespace() helper
2. Apply at IDGenerator namespace input and CLI --namespace input
3. Single source of truth for the rule

Lens: hardcoded.

description:

PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND = 'dcat prime --replay'
PRECOMPACT_HOOK_RECORD = {'matcher': '', 'hooks': [{'type': 'command', 'command': PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND}]}

Lives in constants.py or a new src/dogcat/services/claude_hooks.py (which would also be the right home for the upcoming dcat-48sy hook commands).

Standardize on 'namespace' (drop 'prefix'/'issue_prefix' synonyms)
description:
Three names for the same concept:
- The user-facing config key migrated from issue_prefix to namespace (config.py:445 migrate_config_keys)

Fix: rename helpers to get_namespace, set_namespace, extract_namespace, _detect_namespace_from_*. Rename IDGenerator.prefix parameter to namespace. Keep old names as deprecation shims for one release.

Rename cli/_validate.validate_issue to validate_issue_record (collision)
description:
Two functions named validate_issue with different semantics:

- src/dogcat/models.py:383 validate_issue(issue: Issue) -> None (raises on bad object state)
- src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:88 validate_issue(record: dict, lineno: int) -> list[dict] (returns errors for a JSONL line)

Both imported by tests; a future module importing both collides.

Fix: rename cli/_validate.validate_issue → validate_issue_record. Mirrors validate_proposal_record already in same file at line 297. Symmetric and self-documenting.

Lens: naming.

description:
src/dogcat/event_log.py:101-139: EventLog.emit best-effort wrapper swallows OSError/RuntimeError at DEBUG. The name 'emit' suggests synchronous commitment; callers don't know it can silently fail.

Lens: naming + error-gaps.

Type web app.state via ProposeAppState dataclass
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:67-71 writes 5 attributes magically; routes.py:118,179,234 read via getattr (with default that's WRONG — see Red item dogcat-kz1a).

Fix:

@dataclass
class ProposeAppState:
    dogcats_dir: str
    namespace: str
    namespaces: list[str]
    allow_creating_namespaces: bool
    templates: Jinja2Templates

Mount on app.state.dcat. Provide typed accessor get_state(request: Request) -> ProposeAppState.

Eliminates the getattr default-drift issue completely.

description:

Add ConcurrentEditWarning + ConcurrentFieldDiff dataclasses
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:443-540 (detect_concurrent_edits) returns list[dict[str, Any]] with nested {level, message, issue_id, fields: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]}. Inner fields dict is itself {field_name: {base, branch_1, branch_2}}.

Doctor's pretty-printer at _cmd_doctor.py:531-539 accesses warn['message'], warn.get('fields', {}), diff['branch_1'], diff['branch_2'] — all stringly-typed.

Replace with:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ConcurrentFieldDiff:
    base: Any
    branch_1: Any
    branch_2: Any

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ConcurrentEditWarning:
    level: Literal['warning']
    message: str
    issue_id: str
    fields: dict[str, ConcurrentFieldDiff]

Lens: type-structs.

Promote CSRF cookie + CSP constants out of inline route code
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:21,80-82 has:
- CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE = 3600 (1 hour, magic literal)
- CSP string inline inside SecurityHeadersMiddleware.dispatch: 'default-src 'none'; style-src 'self'; script-src 'self''

CSP is a security policy. Inlining it inside an inner-class dispatch makes it hard to audit, hard to test, and easy to drift if a sibling endpoint is added.

Fix: promote to constants.py:
- CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS = 60 * 60 (named for the unit)
- WEB_CSP_HEADER = 'default-src ...' (audit-friendly)

CSRF_COOKIE_NAME already at module level — keep there or also move.

Lens: hardcoded.

prune_tombstones leaks file handle (no context manager)
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:1518 opens the JSONL file with `for raw_line in self.path.open("rb"):` — handle never closed. Every other site uses `with`.

Fix: `with self.path.open("rb") as src:`.

Lens: error-gaps (High)

title: prune_tombstones leaks file handle (no context manager)
typing_extensions is undeclared runtime dependency
description:
src/dogcat/models.py:9 imports `from typing_extensions import Self`. Not in pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]. Currently works only because textual transitively pulls it in. If textual ever drops the dep, dcat breaks at import time.

Fix: add `typing_extensions>=4.0.0` to dependencies. Optionally use stdlib `typing.Self` for Python>=3.11 with a fallback.

Lens: dep-hygiene (High)

title: typing_extensions is undeclared runtime dependency
Web allow_creating_namespaces getattr default flips security stance
description:
Both call sites use `getattr(state, "allow_creating_namespaces", True)` which defaults to True when the attribute is missing — opposite of create_app's default of False. A bare app construction silently allows namespace minting.

src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:119, 235

Change defaults to False. Add a missing-attribute test.

Lens: test-gaps + error-gaps (High)

title: Web allow_creating_namespaces getattr default flips security stance
check_id_uniqueness is a no-op that misleads doctor
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:1461-1470: function body unconditionally returns True. Docstring acknowledges "Since _issues is a dict, IDs are unique by construction". The dcat doctor 'Issue ID uniqueness' check at _cmd_doctor.py:313 provides false assurance.

Either implement a real check (scan JSONL for duplicate hashes — duplicates can sneak in via concurrent merges or hand-edits) or delete and remove the doctor call.

Lens: dead-code (High)

title: check_id_uniqueness is a no-op that misleads doctor
Advisory file lock has no timeout
description:
fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) blocks indefinitely. A stale dcat process holding the lock blocks every future write forever.

src/dogcat/locking.py:32

Add LOCK_NB retry loop with default ~30s timeout. On exhaustion, raise RuntimeError naming the lock path so user can investigate.

Lens: error-gaps (High)

title: Advisory file lock has no timeout
Compaction silently drops malformed event records
description:
_save_locked re-reads source file to preserve event records and silently skips parse failures (`except (orjson.JSONDecodeError, ValueError): continue`). Corrupt events vanish on routine compaction with no log.

src/dogcat/storage.py:341-344

At minimum log a warning. Ideally refuse compaction when source has unparseable lines so user can recover from git.

Lens: error-gaps (Critical)

title: Compaction silently drops malformed event records
InboxStorage silently tolerates malformed mid-file lines
description:
JSONLStorage._load only tolerates a malformed *last* line (storage.py:197-208). InboxStorage._load skips any malformed line silently and sets _needs_compaction (inbox.py:96-103). Mid-file proposal corruption vanishes silently on next compaction.

Pick one policy. The strict one (storage.py) is safer for an audit log. If keeping inbox lenient, document why and surface _needs_compaction via dcat doctor before compaction silently rewrites.

Lens: error-gaps + test-gaps (High)

title: InboxStorage silently tolerates malformed mid-file lines
inbox accept cross-storage flow has no rollback
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py:530-538: storage.create(issue) succeeds, then remote_inbox.close(...) runs separately. If close fails (network, lock, OSError), the local issue exists but the remote proposal is never closed and gets no resolved_issue link — orphaned data.

Extract to services/inbox_promote.py:promote_to_issue(...) and add partial-failure test (monkey-patch close to raise; assert error surfaces and detect orphaned local issue).

Lens: extract-logic + test-gaps (High)

title: inbox accept cross-storage flow has no rollback

Assert CSRF cookie max_age in test_web_propose
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:96-104 (_issue_csrf_token) sets max_age=CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE (3600s) but tests/test_web_propose.py only checks HttpOnly and samesite=strict — no Max-Age assertion.

A regression silently extending session lifetime would slip through.

Suggested: assert 'Max-Age=3600' in the Set-Cookie header.

Lens: test-gaps (High).

title: Assert CSRF cookie max_age in test_web_propose
_is_default_branch treats permission errors as 'safe to compact'
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:399-416: Any non-zero git returncode (permission denied, lock contention, internal error) falls through to `return True` which means "compact freely". The intent is "only compact on default branch to avoid noisy feature-branch diffs". Permission errors silently disable the protection.

Distinguish FileNotFoundError (genuinely no git) from non-zero returncode (git ran, said something). For non-zero, return False (do NOT compact) and log stderr.

Add test that monkey-patches subprocess.run to return non-zero with stderr.

Lens: error-gaps (Medium)

title: _is_default_branch treats permission errors as 'safe to compact'
Log warning on config.toml parse failure (currently silent)
description:
src/dogcat/config.py:89-97 — _load_toml catches (TOMLDecodeError, OSError) and returns {}. A typo in config.toml produces 'all defaults' with no warning. The user spends time wondering why their namespace='foo' setting doesn't apply.

dcat doctor's config_toml check verifies existence only, not parseability — so the file passes the doctor check while still being broken.

Fix:
1. logger.warning on TOMLDecodeError so LOGLEVEL=WARNING surfaces it.
2. dcat doctor runs _load_toml separately and reports parse errors as a distinct check.

Lens: error-gaps.

title: Log warning on config.toml parse failure (currently silent)
Doctor writes settings.json with non-atomic write_text
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:660-664, 685-687: _install_precompact_hook and _upgrade_precompact_hook use stdlib json.dumps + settings_path.write_text(...) — only place in the codebase using direct write_text on a settings file. No atomic rename, no file lock.

Two concurrent dcat doctor --fix runs (or doctor + Claude Code modifying the same file) could clobber each other or produce a partial file on a crash. Inconsistent with project's own discipline (atomic_rewrite_jsonl pattern used everywhere else).

Fix: reuse atomic_rewrite_jsonl-style write (write to temp, fsync, replace). Even a shallow helper would suffice. While at it, replace stdlib json with orjson(option=OPT_INDENT_2|OPT_APPEND_NEWLINE) for consistency.

Lens: error-gaps.

title: Doctor writes settings.json with non-atomic write_text

Add tests for doctor --fix paths (dangling deps, issue_prefix migration)
description:
Two doctor --fix paths are unit-tested in their helpers but not in the doctor wiring:

1. Dangling deps fix at src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:361-373 — calls storage.remove_dependencies. tests/test_cmd_doctor.py has zero coverage (grep for 'dangling_deps' / 'remove_dependencies' returns nothing).
   Suggested test: build JSONL with a dependency referencing a tombstoned issue's full_id, run doctor --fix, assert dep is gone and file is rewritten.

2. issue_prefix → namespace migration at src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:178-192 — calls migrate_config_keys. tests/test_config.py:475+ tests the helper, but the doctor --fix wiring isn't covered.
   Suggested test: write config.toml with deprecated key, run doctor --fix, assert namespace is set and issue_prefix is gone.

Lens: test-gaps (High).

title: Add tests for doctor --fix paths (dangling deps, issue_prefix migration)
Add stream watchdog Observer integration tests
description:
src/dogcat/stream.py:170-205, 405-414, 538-565 — StreamEmitter.on_modified, on_moved, InboxStreamEmitter.on_modified, and StreamWatcher.start/stop are never directly exercised. tests/test_stream.py calls _handle_file_change() directly, bypassing the watchdog dispatch.

The path-filter logic (endswith('issues.jsonl') vs endswith('inbox.jsonl')) is unverified — a typo like endswith('issue.jsonl') would not be caught.

Suggested:
- Synthesize FileModifiedEvent / FileMovedEvent instances with various src_path/dest_path values; assert the right handler runs (or doesn't).
- One integration test that starts/stops the Observer against a real temp file.

Lens: test-gaps (High).

title: Add stream watchdog Observer integration tests
Web app should hold persistent InboxStorage instead of constructing per request
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:79: _create_proposal_sync builds a brand-new InboxStorage(dogcats_dir=...) on every POST. Constructor calls _load() which readlines() the entire inbox file and parses every record.

Per-submit latency grows linearly with inbox size. Concurrent contention with writers.

Fix: hold one InboxStorage on app.state at app boot. Guard with inbox.reload() only when file mtime/size changed (cheap stat check). The lock already serializes writes; reload-on-change keeps memory state fresh.

Same fix applies to namespace enumeration (app.state.namespaces is currently snapshotted at boot and never refreshed — new namespaces minted via CLI invisible until restart).

Lens: query-smells (High).

title: Web app should hold persistent InboxStorage instead of constructing per request
Add stream watchdog Observer integration tests
description:
src/dogcat/stream.py:170-205, 405-414, 538-565 — StreamEmitter.on_modified, on_moved, InboxStreamEmitter.on_modified, and StreamWatcher.start/stop are never directly exercised. tests/test_stream.py calls _handle_file_change() directly, bypassing the watchdog dispatch.

The path-filter logic (endswith('issues.jsonl') vs endswith('inbox.jsonl')) is unverified — a typo like endswith('issue.jsonl') would not be caught.

Suggested:
- Synthesize FileModifiedEvent / FileMovedEvent instances with various src_path/dest_path values; assert the right handler runs (or doesn't).
- One integration test that starts/stops the Observer against a real temp file.

Lens: test-gaps (High).

title: Add stream watchdog Observer integration tests
Web app should hold persistent InboxStorage instead of constructing per request
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:79: _create_proposal_sync builds a brand-new InboxStorage(dogcats_dir=...) on every POST. Constructor calls _load() which readlines() the entire inbox file and parses every record.

Per-submit latency grows linearly with inbox size. Concurrent contention with writers.

Fix: hold one InboxStorage on app.state at app boot. Guard with inbox.reload() only when file mtime/size changed (cheap stat check). The lock already serializes writes; reload-on-change keeps memory state fresh.

Same fix applies to namespace enumeration (app.state.namespaces is currently snapshotted at boot and never refreshed — new namespaces minted via CLI invisible until restart).

Lens: query-smells (High).

title: Web app should hold persistent InboxStorage instead of constructing per request
Merge issue+event records into single locked append (write amplification)
description:
Each storage mutation does TWO file-lock acquisitions and TWO fsyncs to the same file: one for the issue record, one for the event record (via EventLog.emit → EventLog.append which takes its own lock).

Sites: src/dogcat/storage.py:529 (create), 733 (update), 977 (close), 1034 (reopen), 1110 (delete) — each calls _append([record]) followed by _emit_event which goes to event_log.py:101-107.

For a CLI batch like dcat close A B C ... Z (via apply_to_each in _cmd_close.py:80), 26 ids ≈ 52 lock acquisitions and 52 fsyncs.

Fix: have _emit_event accept an open lock context, OR batch issue + event records into one _append([issue_record, event_record]) call covered by one lock and one fsync. Mutation methods already construct both back-to-back.

Lens: query-smells (High).

title: Merge issue+event records into single locked append (write amplification)

description:
12+ subprocess.run(['git', ...]) calls scattered across 6+ files:
- src/dogcat/storage.py:399-416 (_is_default_branch)
- src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:174-294 (get_default_operator, is_gitignored, _find_dogcats_via_worktree)
- src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:396-503 (_git_cmd, _load_issues_at_ref, detect_concurrent_edits)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_diff.py:19-54 (_get_git_root, _get_git_file)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:439-454
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_docs.py:121-1069 (multiple)

_get_git_root and _git_repo_root are duplicate functions under different names.

New src/dogcat/git.py: repo_root, current_branch, show_file, is_path_ignored, user_email, get_config, set_config, add_paths, latest_merge_commit, merge_parents, merge_base.

Centralizing makes test fakes trivial (one mock target instead of 12).

Lens: extract-logic + hardcoded + naming.

Compaction silently drops malformed event records
description:
_save_locked re-reads source file to preserve event records and silently skips parse failures (`except (orjson.JSONDecodeError, ValueError): continue`). Corrupt events vanish on routine compaction with no log.

src/dogcat/storage.py:341-344

At minimum log a warning. Ideally refuse compaction when source has unparseable lines so user can recover from git.

Lens: error-gaps (Critical)
closed_reason: Compaction warns on unparseable lines instead of silently dropping events

title: Compaction silently drops malformed event records
prune_tombstones leaks file handle (no context manager)
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:1518 opens the JSONL file with `for raw_line in self.path.open("rb"):` — handle never closed. Every other site uses `with`.

Fix: `with self.path.open("rb") as src:`.

Lens: error-gaps (High)
closed_reason: prune_tombstones now uses a context manager

title: prune_tombstones leaks file handle (no context manager)
Advisory file lock has no timeout
description:
fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) blocks indefinitely. A stale dcat process holding the lock blocks every future write forever.

src/dogcat/locking.py:32

Add LOCK_NB retry loop with default ~30s timeout. On exhaustion, raise RuntimeError naming the lock path so user can investigate.

Lens: error-gaps (High)
closed_reason: Advisory lock now bounds wait via DCAT_LOCK_TIMEOUT_SECS (default 30s)

title: Advisory file lock has no timeout
Web allow_creating_namespaces getattr default flips security stance
description:
Both call sites use `getattr(state, "allow_creating_namespaces", True)` which defaults to True when the attribute is missing — opposite of create_app's default of False. A bare app construction silently allows namespace minting.

src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:119, 235

Change defaults to False. Add a missing-attribute test.

Lens: test-gaps + error-gaps (High)
closed_reason: getattr defaults flipped to False so missing attribute does not silently allow namespace creation

title: Web allow_creating_namespaces getattr default flips security stance
Add tests for web propose length limits and security headers
description:
Two missing test areas:

1. MAX_TITLE_LEN=500 and MAX_DESC_LEN=50000 enforcement in src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:222-226 has zero coverage. A typo loosening bounds would ship silently. Add boundary tests at limit and limit+1 for both.

2. SecurityHeadersMiddleware at src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:78-79 — only CSP is asserted in test_web_propose.py. X-Content-Type-Options and X-Frame-Options would silently regress.

Lens: test-gaps (Critical)
closed_reason: Added MAX_TITLE_LEN/MAX_DESC_LEN boundary tests + X-Content-Type-Options/X-Frame-Options assertions

title: Add tests for web propose length limits and security headers
InboxStorage silently tolerates malformed mid-file lines
description:
JSONLStorage._load only tolerates a malformed *last* line (storage.py:197-208). InboxStorage._load skips any malformed line silently and sets _needs_compaction (inbox.py:96-103). Mid-file proposal corruption vanishes silently on next compaction.

Pick one policy. The strict one (storage.py) is safer for an audit log. If keeping inbox lenient, document why and surface _needs_compaction via dcat doctor before compaction silently rewrites.

Lens: error-gaps + test-gaps (High)
closed_reason: InboxStorage now matches storage.py: only the last line is tolerated when malformed

title: InboxStorage silently tolerates malformed mid-file lines
inbox accept cross-storage flow has no rollback
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py:530-538: storage.create(issue) succeeds, then remote_inbox.close(...) runs separately. If close fails (network, lock, OSError), the local issue exists but the remote proposal is never closed and gets no resolved_issue link — orphaned data.

Extract to services/inbox_promote.py:promote_to_issue(...) and add partial-failure test (monkey-patch close to raise; assert error surfaces and detect orphaned local issue).

Lens: extract-logic + test-gaps (High)
closed_reason: inbox accept rolls back the local issue if remote close fails

title: inbox accept cross-storage flow has no rollback
typing_extensions is undeclared runtime dependency
description:
src/dogcat/models.py:9 imports `from typing_extensions import Self`. Not in pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]. Currently works only because textual transitively pulls it in. If textual ever drops the dep, dcat breaks at import time.

Fix: add `typing_extensions>=4.0.0` to dependencies. Optionally use stdlib `typing.Self` for Python>=3.11 with a fallback.

Lens: dep-hygiene (High)
closed_reason: typing_extensions declared in pyproject.toml dependencies

title: typing_extensions is undeclared runtime dependency
check_id_uniqueness is a no-op that misleads doctor
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:1461-1470: function body unconditionally returns True. Docstring acknowledges "Since _issues is a dict, IDs are unique by construction". The dcat doctor 'Issue ID uniqueness' check at _cmd_doctor.py:313 provides false assurance.

Either implement a real check (scan JSONL for duplicate hashes — duplicates can sneak in via concurrent merges or hand-edits) or delete and remove the doctor call.

Lens: dead-code (High)
closed_reason: check_id_uniqueness now scans JSONL for hash collisions via differing created_at

title: check_id_uniqueness is a no-op that misleads doctor
Assert CSRF cookie max_age in test_web_propose
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:96-104 (_issue_csrf_token) sets max_age=CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE (3600s) but tests/test_web_propose.py only checks HttpOnly and samesite=strict — no Max-Age assertion.

A regression silently extending session lifetime would slip through.

Suggested: assert 'Max-Age=3600' in the Set-Cookie header.

Lens: test-gaps (High).
closed_reason: Added Max-Age=CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE assertion to test_web_propose

title: Assert CSRF cookie max_age in test_web_propose
_is_default_branch treats permission errors as 'safe to compact'
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:399-416: Any non-zero git returncode (permission denied, lock contention, internal error) falls through to `return True` which means "compact freely". The intent is "only compact on default branch to avoid noisy feature-branch diffs". Permission errors silently disable the protection.

Distinguish FileNotFoundError (genuinely no git) from non-zero returncode (git ran, said something). For non-zero, return False (do NOT compact) and log stderr.

Add test that monkey-patches subprocess.run to return non-zero with stderr.

Lens: error-gaps (Medium)
closed_reason: Non-zero git rc with non-'no repo' stderr now blocks compaction and logs a warning

title: _is_default_branch treats permission errors as 'safe to compact'
Log warning on config.toml parse failure (currently silent)
description:
src/dogcat/config.py:89-97 — _load_toml catches (TOMLDecodeError, OSError) and returns {}. A typo in config.toml produces 'all defaults' with no warning. The user spends time wondering why their namespace='foo' setting doesn't apply.

dcat doctor's config_toml check verifies existence only, not parseability — so the file passes the doctor check while still being broken.

Fix:
1. logger.warning on TOMLDecodeError so LOGLEVEL=WARNING surfaces it.
2. dcat doctor runs _load_toml separately and reports parse errors as a distinct check.

Lens: error-gaps.
closed_reason: _load_toml warns on TOMLDecodeError and doctor adds config_toml_parseable check

title: Log warning on config.toml parse failure (currently silent)
Doctor writes settings.json with non-atomic write_text
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:660-664, 685-687: _install_precompact_hook and _upgrade_precompact_hook use stdlib json.dumps + settings_path.write_text(...) — only place in the codebase using direct write_text on a settings file. No atomic rename, no file lock.

Two concurrent dcat doctor --fix runs (or doctor + Claude Code modifying the same file) could clobber each other or produce a partial file on a crash. Inconsistent with project's own discipline (atomic_rewrite_jsonl pattern used everywhere else).

Fix: reuse atomic_rewrite_jsonl-style write (write to temp, fsync, replace). Even a shallow helper would suffice. While at it, replace stdlib json with orjson(option=OPT_INDENT_2|OPT_APPEND_NEWLINE) for consistency.

Lens: error-gaps.
closed_reason: Doctor settings.json writes use atomic temp+fsync+replace via _atomic_write_json

title: Doctor writes settings.json with non-atomic write_text
Add tests for doctor --fix paths (dangling deps, issue_prefix migration)
description:
Two doctor --fix paths are unit-tested in their helpers but not in the doctor wiring:

1. Dangling deps fix at src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:361-373 — calls storage.remove_dependencies. tests/test_cmd_doctor.py has zero coverage (grep for 'dangling_deps' / 'remove_dependencies' returns nothing).
   Suggested test: build JSONL with a dependency referencing a tombstoned issue's full_id, run doctor --fix, assert dep is gone and file is rewritten.

2. issue_prefix → namespace migration at src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:178-192 — calls migrate_config_keys. tests/test_config.py:475+ tests the helper, but the doctor --fix wiring isn't covered.
   Suggested test: write config.toml with deprecated key, run doctor --fix, assert namespace is set and issue_prefix is gone.

Lens: test-gaps (High).
closed_reason: Added doctor --fix tests for dangling deps and issue_prefix→namespace migration

Refactor doctor's 14+ inline check dicts into Check dataclass list
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:64-432 builds a flat dict[str, dict[str, Any]] via 14+ repeated checks[name] = {description, passed, fix, fail_description, optional, note} blocks plus if not passed: all_passed = False after each.

Add @dataclass DoctorCheck and convert to list[DoctorCheck]. Each check becomes a function returning a Check. Body shrinks from ~520 lines to ~50 lines of orchestration. Individual checks become unit-testable.

Lens: complexity + extract-logic + simplify-code + type-structs (4-reviewer consensus).

title: Refactor doctor's 14+ inline check dicts into Check dataclass list
Refactor doctor's 14+ inline check dicts into Check dataclass list
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:64-432 builds a flat dict[str, dict[str, Any]] via 14+ repeated checks[name] = {description, passed, fix, fail_description, optional, note} blocks plus if not passed: all_passed = False after each.

Add @dataclass DoctorCheck and convert to list[DoctorCheck]. Each check becomes a function returning a Check. Body shrinks from ~520 lines to ~50 lines of orchestration. Individual checks become unit-testable.

Lens: complexity + extract-logic + simplify-code + type-structs (4-reviewer consensus).

title: Refactor doctor's 14+ inline check dicts into Check dataclass list
Route workflow list-style commands through existing _list_by_status helper
description:
Seven public list-style commands open-code the same pipeline (~50-line bodies + ~85 lines of identical Typer.Option declarations each = ~800-950 duplicated lines).

src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py: ready (43-191), in_progress (367-496), open_issues (498-627), in_review (629-758), deferred (1082-1211), manual_list (1239-1350), snoozed_list (1919-2061).

Helper _list_by_status already exists at lines 796-868 but is only used by hidden ir/ip aliases. Migrate the public commands to delegate to it. Also: replace recently_closed_alias and recently_added_alias (1611-1658, 1672-1719) with _make_alias calls (mirrors o/b/d aliases at 1660-1670).

Single biggest duplication in the codebase. Lens: duplicates + extract-logic + simplify-code + complexity (4-reviewer consensus).

title: Route workflow list-style commands through existing _list_by_status helper
Single PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND constant (eliminate 5 string copies)
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py duplicates 'dcat prime --replay' string 5 times: lines 392, 396, 650, 656, 682. The replacement at line 682 does cmd.replace('dcat prime', 'dcat prime --replay') which is fragile if 'dcat prime' ever appears as a substring.

Fix: single constants:

PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND = 'dcat prime --replay'
PRECOMPACT_HOOK_RECORD = {'matcher': '', 'hooks': [{'type': 'command', 'command': PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND}]}

Lives in constants.py or a new src/dogcat/services/claude_hooks.py (which would also be the right home for the upcoming dcat-48sy hook commands).

Lens: hardcoded.

title: Single PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND constant (eliminate 5 string copies)
Centralize JSONL filename literals in constants.py
description:
Filenames .dogcats, issues.jsonl, inbox.jsonl, .issues.lock are repeated across 10+ files:

- storage.py:46,84 - .dogcats/issues.jsonl, .issues.lock
- inbox.py:36,58 - .dogcats, .issues.lock
- event_log.py:99,185,191 - .issues.lock, issues.jsonl, inbox.jsonl
- web/propose/__init__.py:26,48 - .dogcats, issues.jsonl
- cli/_cmd_inbox.py - .dogcats x6
- stream.py:195,204,407,412,523,548 - issues.jsonl, inbox.jsonl
- cli/_cmd_doctor.py:568, _cmd_init.py:226, _cmd_docs.py:160,174

inbox.py defines INBOX_FILENAME and config.py defines DOGCATS_DIR_NAME, but event_log redefines as class attributes; storage default args hardcode again.

Fix: promote ISSUES_FILENAME = 'issues.jsonl' and LOCK_FILENAME = '.issues.lock' to constants.py. Have JSONLStorage default join from DOGCATS_DIR_NAME + ISSUES_FILENAME.

Lens: hardcoded.

title: Centralize JSONL filename literals in constants.py
Promote _DEFAULT_BRANCHES to constants and respect git's init.defaultBranch
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:397: _DEFAULT_BRANCHES = frozenset({'main', 'master'}) is a class-attribute set used to gate auto-compaction. A project using develop, trunk, or a custom default branch loses auto-compaction silently.

Modern git installs respect init.defaultBranch which can be queried with git config --get init.defaultBranch.

Fix:
1. Move to constants.py as DEFAULT_BRANCH_NAMES
2. _is_default_branch should also read init.defaultBranch from git config and union with the hardcoded fallback

Note: related to dcat-49nq (allow compaction on non-default branches) but distinct — that one is about removing the gate; this is about making the gate config-aware.

Lens: hardcoded.

title: Promote _DEFAULT_BRANCHES to constants and respect git's init.defaultBranch
Move web validation limits + namespace pattern to constants.py (share with CLI)
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:25-32 has MAX_TITLE_LEN=500, MAX_DESC_LEN=50000, MAX_NAMESPACE_LEN=64, NAMESPACE_PATTERN regex. These are the ONLY namespace shape rules in the codebase, but namespaces flow through idgen.py, storage.py, inbox.py, config.py (pinned_namespaces), and CLI (--namespace).

CLI accepts any string today; web rejects via regex. Inconsistent enforcement.

Fix:
1. Promote to constants.py with is_valid_namespace() helper
2. Apply at IDGenerator namespace input and CLI --namespace input
3. Single source of truth for the rule

Lens: hardcoded.

title: Move web validation limits + namespace pattern to constants.py (share with CLI)
Promote CSRF cookie + CSP constants out of inline route code
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:21,80-82 has:
- CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE = 3600 (1 hour, magic literal)
- CSP string inline inside SecurityHeadersMiddleware.dispatch: 'default-src 'none'; style-src 'self'; script-src 'self''

CSP is a security policy. Inlining it inside an inner-class dispatch makes it hard to audit, hard to test, and easy to drift if a sibling endpoint is added.

Fix: promote to constants.py:
- CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS = 60 * 60 (named for the unit)
- WEB_CSP_HEADER = 'default-src ...' (audit-friendly)

CSRF_COOKIE_NAME already at module level — keep there or also move.

Lens: hardcoded.

title: Promote CSRF cookie + CSP constants out of inline route code
