Rename cli/_validate.validate_issue to validate_issue_record (collision)
description:
Two functions named validate_issue with different semantics:

- src/dogcat/models.py:383 validate_issue(issue: Issue) -> None (raises on bad object state)
- src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:88 validate_issue(record: dict, lineno: int) -> list[dict] (returns errors for a JSONL line)

Both imported by tests; a future module importing both collides.

Fix: rename cli/_validate.validate_issue → validate_issue_record. Mirrors validate_proposal_record already in same file at line 297. Symmetric and self-documenting.

Lens: naming.

title: Rename cli/_validate.validate_issue to validate_issue_record (collision)
Rename EventLog.emit to try_emit (lies about swallow contract)
description:
src/dogcat/event_log.py:101-139: EventLog.emit best-effort wrapper swallows OSError/RuntimeError at DEBUG. The name 'emit' suggests synchronous commitment; callers don't know it can silently fail.

Fix: rename to try_emit or emit_best_effort with a docstring spelling out the swallow contract. EventLog.append (the underlying file write) keeps its name.

Lens: naming + error-gaps.

title: Rename EventLog.emit to try_emit (lies about swallow contract)
Replace inline _field_value reimplementation with _diff.field_value import
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_diff.py:145-149 reimplements field_value inline (5 lines). Three other modules (storage.py, _validate.py, plus _diff.py itself) all delegate or reimplement.

_diff.field_value is the canonical home (storage.py:15 already imports it).

Fix: replace cli/_cmd_diff.py inline copy with from dogcat._diff import field_value. Optionally drop the thin delegators in storage.py:437-440 and cli/_validate.py:436-440 (they add no value).

Lens: duplicates + simplify-code.

title: Replace inline _field_value reimplementation with _diff.field_value import
Web POST mutates app.state.namespaces before write succeeds
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:241: valid_namespaces.append(namespace) is called BEFORE the asyncio.to_thread(_create_proposal_sync, ...) write. If the write fails, the namespace is still in valid_namespaces for the rest of the process lifetime. Subsequent submissions to that namespace bypass the 'is_new_namespace' branch (they appear to exist) and won't be persisted to pinned_namespaces.

Fix: append only after successful _create_proposal_sync (move next to the existing _persist_pinned_namespace call).

Lens: error-gaps.

title: Web POST mutates app.state.namespaces before write succeeds
Type apply_common_filters as list[Issue] (not list[Any])
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:349-436 (apply_common_filters), :614,635,652,686 (proposal helpers): all type their lists as list[Any] and storage as Any. Function bodies assume the full Issue / Proposal shape (issue_type.value, priority, labels, owner, parent, metadata, comments, full_id, namespace).

list[Any] defeats pyright strict mode. ~25 callers across cli/_cmd_*.py.

Fix: type as list[Issue] / list[Proposal] and JSONLStorage | None (forward-ref to avoid circular imports).

Lens: type-structs.

Add JSONLStorage._resolve_or_raise helper (19 inline copies)
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py has the 4-line block:

    resolved_id = self.resolve_id(issue_id)
    if resolved_id is None:
        msg = f'Issue {issue_id} not found'
        raise ValueError(msg)

repeated ~19 times: lines 1145, 1150, 1197, 1202, 1238, 1256, 1283, 1288, 1324, 1329, 1361, 1379, 1397 plus several earlier methods (close, reopen, delete).

Add JSONLStorage._resolve_or_raise(self, issue_id, *, label='Issue') -> str. Replaces ~95 lines with single-line calls. Mirror in InboxStorage for proposal lookups (close, delete, get).

Lens: duplicates.

title: Add JSONLStorage._resolve_or_raise helper (19 inline copies)
Add require_resolved_id helper (6 inline 'resolve or echo+exit' copies)
description:
Pattern repeats in cli/_cmd_create.py:313-338 (×4: depends_on, blocks, duplicate_of, parent) and cli/_cmd_update.py:243-260 (×2: duplicate_of, parent):

if foo:
    resolved = storage.resolve_id(foo)
    if resolved is None:
        echo_error(f'... {foo} not found')
        raise typer.Exit(1)
    foo = resolved

Fix: cli/_helpers.py:require_resolved_id(storage, raw_id, *, label='Issue') -> str that raises typer.BadParameter or echoes+exits. Six inline copies become six one-liners.

Lens: extract-logic.

title: Add require_resolved_id helper (6 inline 'resolve or echo+exit' copies)
Extract set_manual_flag helper (duplicated metadata mutation)
description:
Toggle-manual metadata mutation duplicated 3 places:
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_update.py:289-302 (_update closure)
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1379-1385 (mark_manual_shortcut)
- src/dogcat/tui/detail_panel.py:799-808 (_do_update)

All do dict(current.metadata), set/pop 'manual', pop legacy 'no_agent', then assign back. The legacy no_agent cleanup is a business rule that lives in three places.

Fix: models.py set_manual_flag(metadata: dict, manual: bool) -> dict next to is_manual_issue. Or JSONLStorage.set_manual(issue_id, manual, *, updated_by) -> Issue so storage layer owns the mutation atomically.

Lens: extract-logic.

title: Extract set_manual_flag helper (duplicated metadata mutation)
Replace status string literals with Status enum
description:
Raw 'in_progress', 'open', 'in_review', 'deferred' literals when filtering, plus tuple membership checks ('closed', 'tombstone') bypass the Status enum.

src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:453, 584, 715, 1168, 1310, 1566, 1756, 1765, 2016

Fix: use Status.IN_PROGRESS.value etc. at every call site. Add TERMINAL_STATUSES = frozenset({Status.CLOSED.value, Status.TOMBSTONE.value}) constant. Optionally change storage.list() to accept Status enum members directly.

Lens: hardcoded.

Add @cli_command decorator for try/except typer.Exit envelope
description:
Pattern try: ... except typer.Exit: raise; except Exception as e: echo_error(str(e)); raise typer.Exit(1) repeats in 45+ command bodies across cli/_cmd_*.py.

Single decorator wrapper applied to each @app.command() removes ~180 lines and shallows command bodies by 4 levels.

Lens: simplify-code + duplicates.

title: Add @cli_command decorator for try/except typer.Exit envelope
Extract _cascade_id_rename helper for change_namespace's 3 cascade loops
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:737-841 (change_namespace, ~80 lines) has 3 near-identical cascade loops: other issues' parent/duplicate_of refs, dependencies, links. Each loop walks the corresponding collection looking for old_full_id and rewrites.

Extract _cascade_id_rename(old_id, new_id) -> list[dict] returning all records-to-append for issues/deps/links cascade. The three loops collapse into one helper. change_namespace body shrinks to ~30 lines.

Lens: complexity + duplicates.

title: Extract _cascade_id_rename helper for change_namespace's 3 cascade loops
Type web app.state via ProposeAppState dataclass
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:67-71 writes 5 attributes magically; routes.py:118,179,234 read via getattr (with default that's WRONG — see Red item dogcat-kz1a).

Fix:

@dataclass
class ProposeAppState:
    dogcats_dir: str
    namespace: str
    namespaces: list[str]
    allow_creating_namespaces: bool
    templates: Jinja2Templates

Mount on app.state.dcat. Provide typed accessor get_state(request: Request) -> ProposeAppState.

Eliminates the getattr default-drift issue completely.

Lens: type-structs.

title: Type web app.state via ProposeAppState dataclass
Add ConcurrentEditWarning + ConcurrentFieldDiff dataclasses
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:443-540 (detect_concurrent_edits) returns list[dict[str, Any]] with nested {level, message, issue_id, fields: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]}. Inner fields dict is itself {field_name: {base, branch_1, branch_2}}.

Doctor's pretty-printer at _cmd_doctor.py:531-539 accesses warn['message'], warn.get('fields', {}), diff['branch_1'], diff['branch_2'] — all stringly-typed.

Replace with:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ConcurrentFieldDiff:
    base: Any
    branch_1: Any
    branch_2: Any

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ConcurrentEditWarning:
    level: Literal['warning']
    message: str
    issue_id: str
    fields: dict[str, ConcurrentFieldDiff]

Lens: type-structs.

title: Add ConcurrentEditWarning + ConcurrentFieldDiff dataclasses
Convert validation errors from list[dict[str,str]] to ValidationError dataclass
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:39-388 — 12+ functions return list[dict[str, str]] with implicit {level, message} shape. Consumed by cli/_cmd_doctor.py:66, 247-271, 471-487 which does e['level'] == 'error' string comparisons.

Replace with:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ValidationError:
    level: Literal['error', 'warning']
    message: str

Bump signatures: list[dict[str, str]] → list[ValidationError]. Doctor JSON output stays identical via dataclasses.asdict.

Pyright catches typo'd e['levle']; Literal rules out third-state bugs.

Lens: type-structs.

title: Convert validation errors from list[dict[str,str]] to ValidationError dataclass
Add @cli_command decorator for try/except typer.Exit envelope
description:
Pattern try: ... except typer.Exit: raise; except Exception as e: echo_error(str(e)); raise typer.Exit(1) repeats in 45+ command bodies across cli/_cmd_*.py.

Single decorator wrapper applied to each @app.command() removes ~180 lines and shallows command bodies by 4 levels.

Lens: simplify-code + duplicates.

title: Add @cli_command decorator for try/except typer.Exit envelope
Extract _cascade_id_rename helper for change_namespace's 3 cascade loops
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:737-841 (change_namespace, ~80 lines) has 3 near-identical cascade loops: other issues' parent/duplicate_of refs, dependencies, links. Each loop walks the corresponding collection looking for old_full_id and rewrites.

Extract _cascade_id_rename(old_id, new_id) -> list[dict] returning all records-to-append for issues/deps/links cascade. The three loops collapse into one helper. change_namespace body shrinks to ~30 lines.

Lens: complexity + duplicates.

title: Extract _cascade_id_rename helper for change_namespace's 3 cascade loops
Type web app.state via ProposeAppState dataclass
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:67-71 writes 5 attributes magically; routes.py:118,179,234 read via getattr (with default that's WRONG — see Red item dogcat-kz1a).

Fix:

@dataclass
class ProposeAppState:
    dogcats_dir: str
    namespace: str
    namespaces: list[str]
    allow_creating_namespaces: bool
    templates: Jinja2Templates

Mount on app.state.dcat. Provide typed accessor get_state(request: Request) -> ProposeAppState.

Eliminates the getattr default-drift issue completely.

Lens: type-structs.

title: Type web app.state via ProposeAppState dataclass
Add ConcurrentEditWarning + ConcurrentFieldDiff dataclasses
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:443-540 (detect_concurrent_edits) returns list[dict[str, Any]] with nested {level, message, issue_id, fields: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]}. Inner fields dict is itself {field_name: {base, branch_1, branch_2}}.

Doctor's pretty-printer at _cmd_doctor.py:531-539 accesses warn['message'], warn.get('fields', {}), diff['branch_1'], diff['branch_2'] — all stringly-typed.

Replace with:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ConcurrentFieldDiff:
    base: Any
    branch_1: Any
    branch_2: Any

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ConcurrentEditWarning:
    level: Literal['warning']
    message: str
    issue_id: str
    fields: dict[str, ConcurrentFieldDiff]

Lens: type-structs.

title: Add ConcurrentEditWarning + ConcurrentFieldDiff dataclasses
Convert validation errors from list[dict[str,str]] to ValidationError dataclass
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:39-388 — 12+ functions return list[dict[str, str]] with implicit {level, message} shape. Consumed by cli/_cmd_doctor.py:66, 247-271, 471-487 which does e['level'] == 'error' string comparisons.

Replace with:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ValidationError:
    level: Literal['error', 'warning']
    message: str

Bump signatures: list[dict[str, str]] → list[ValidationError]. Doctor JSON output stays identical via dataclasses.asdict.

Pyright catches typo'd e['levle']; Literal rules out third-state bugs.

Lens: type-structs.

title: Convert validation errors from list[dict[str,str]] to ValidationError dataclass
Refactor JSONLStorage._load: extract per-record-type parsers
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:96-230 — _load is 135 lines, 6 levels deep. Multi-record-type dispatch (link/dependency/event/issue) × add/remove ops, plus version tracking + last-line tolerance + warning emission. The if rtype == 'link' and elif rtype == 'dependency' blocks are nearly identical structurally.

Extract:
- _parse_link_record(data, link_map) -> None
- _parse_dependency_record(data, dep_map) -> None
- _parse_issue_record(data, issues) -> None

Hot loop becomes a dispatch table: parsers = {'link': _parse_link_record, ...}. Function shrinks to ~50 lines.

Lens: complexity.

title: Refactor JSONLStorage._load: extract per-record-type parsers

Refactor _create_impl: extract resolve_overrides/references/dependencies helpers
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_create.py:71-396 — _create_impl is ~190 lines of body with 14+ branches: shorthand-vs-flag conflict (×3), title required, body/description merge, label parsing, priority resolution (3-way), type resolution (3-way), status resolution (3-way), dependency target validation (×2), duplicate_of resolution, parent resolution, then create + post-create dependency adds.

Extract three pure helpers:
- _resolve_create_overrides(...) — combine shorthand + flag for priority/issue_type/status
- _resolve_create_references(storage, *, depends_on, blocks, duplicate_of, parent) — resolve all four ID refs
- _apply_create_dependencies(storage, issue, depends_on, blocks, by) — wraps post-create dep adds

Body shrinks to ~80 lines.

Lens: complexity + extract-logic.

description:
Issue creation pattern "namespace lookup → IDGenerator → generate_issue_id → build Issue → storage.create" reimplemented 4 times:

- src/dogcat/demo.py:82-87 (slim version)

src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:64-432 builds a flat dict[str, dict[str, Any]] via 14+ repeated checks[name] = {description, passed, fix, fail_description, optional, note} blocks plus if not passed: all_passed = False after each.

Route workflow list-style commands through existing _list_by_status helper
description:
Seven public list-style commands open-code the same pipeline (~50-line bodies + ~85 lines of identical Typer.Option declarations each = ~800-950 duplicated lines).

src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py: ready (43-191), in_progress (367-496), open_issues (498-627), in_review (629-758), deferred (1082-1211), manual_list (1239-1350), snoozed_list (1919-2061).

Helper _list_by_status already exists at lines 796-868 but is only used by hidden ir/ip aliases. Migrate the public commands to delegate to it. Also: replace recently_closed_alias and recently_added_alias (1611-1658, 1672-1719) with _make_alias calls (mirrors o/b/d aliases at 1660-1670).

Single biggest duplication in the codebase. Lens: duplicates + extract-logic + simplify-code + complexity (4-reviewer consensus).

Single PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND constant (eliminate 5 string copies)
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py duplicates 'dcat prime --replay' string 5 times: lines 392, 396, 650, 656, 682. The replacement at line 682 does cmd.replace('dcat prime', 'dcat prime --replay') which is fragile if 'dcat prime' ever appears as a substring.

Fix: single constants:

PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND = 'dcat prime --replay'
PRECOMPACT_HOOK_RECORD = {'matcher': '', 'hooks': [{'type': 'command', 'command': PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND}]}

Lives in constants.py or a new src/dogcat/services/claude_hooks.py (which would also be the right home for the upcoming dcat-48sy hook commands).

Lens: hardcoded.
closed_reason: PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND + PRECOMPACT_HOOK_RECORD live in constants.py; doctor install/upgrade paths read from there; upgrade no longer uses fragile substring replace

title: Single PRECOMPACT_HOOK_COMMAND constant (eliminate 5 string copies)
Centralize JSONL filename literals in constants.py
description:
Filenames .dogcats, issues.jsonl, inbox.jsonl, .issues.lock are repeated across 10+ files:

- storage.py:46,84 - .dogcats/issues.jsonl, .issues.lock
- inbox.py:36,58 - .dogcats, .issues.lock
- event_log.py:99,185,191 - .issues.lock, issues.jsonl, inbox.jsonl
- web/propose/__init__.py:26,48 - .dogcats, issues.jsonl
- cli/_cmd_inbox.py - .dogcats x6
- stream.py:195,204,407,412,523,548 - issues.jsonl, inbox.jsonl
- cli/_cmd_doctor.py:568, _cmd_init.py:226, _cmd_docs.py:160,174

inbox.py defines INBOX_FILENAME and config.py defines DOGCATS_DIR_NAME, but event_log redefines as class attributes; storage default args hardcode again.

Fix: promote ISSUES_FILENAME = 'issues.jsonl' and LOCK_FILENAME = '.issues.lock' to constants.py. Have JSONLStorage default join from DOGCATS_DIR_NAME + ISSUES_FILENAME.

Lens: hardcoded.
closed_reason: ISSUES_FILENAME/INBOX_FILENAME/LOCK_FILENAME/DOGCATS_DIR_NAME centralized; storage, inbox, event_log, web propose factory all consume them

title: Centralize JSONL filename literals in constants.py
Promote _DEFAULT_BRANCHES to constants and respect git's init.defaultBranch
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:397: _DEFAULT_BRANCHES = frozenset({'main', 'master'}) is a class-attribute set used to gate auto-compaction. A project using develop, trunk, or a custom default branch loses auto-compaction silently.

Modern git installs respect init.defaultBranch which can be queried with git config --get init.defaultBranch.

Fix:
1. Move to constants.py as DEFAULT_BRANCH_NAMES
2. _is_default_branch should also read init.defaultBranch from git config and union with the hardcoded fallback

Note: related to dcat-49nq (allow compaction on non-default branches) but distinct — that one is about removing the gate; this is about making the gate config-aware.

Lens: hardcoded.
closed_reason: DEFAULT_BRANCH_NAMES in constants; storage._is_default_branch unions in init.defaultBranch via git.get_config so non-conventional defaults aren't silently treated as feature branches

title: Promote _DEFAULT_BRANCHES to constants and respect git's init.defaultBranch
Move web validation limits + namespace pattern to constants.py (share with CLI)
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:25-32 has MAX_TITLE_LEN=500, MAX_DESC_LEN=50000, MAX_NAMESPACE_LEN=64, NAMESPACE_PATTERN regex. These are the ONLY namespace shape rules in the codebase, but namespaces flow through idgen.py, storage.py, inbox.py, config.py (pinned_namespaces), and CLI (--namespace).

CLI accepts any string today; web rejects via regex. Inconsistent enforcement.

Fix:
1. Promote to constants.py with is_valid_namespace() helper
2. Apply at IDGenerator namespace input and CLI --namespace input
3. Single source of truth for the rule

Lens: hardcoded.
closed_reason: MAX_TITLE_LEN/MAX_DESC_LEN/MAX_NAMESPACE_LEN/NAMESPACE_PATTERN + is_valid_namespace helper in constants; web propose route imports them

title: Move web validation limits + namespace pattern to constants.py (share with CLI)
Promote CSRF cookie + CSP constants out of inline route code
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:21,80-82 has:
- CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE = 3600 (1 hour, magic literal)
- CSP string inline inside SecurityHeadersMiddleware.dispatch: 'default-src 'none'; style-src 'self'; script-src 'self''

CSP is a security policy. Inlining it inside an inner-class dispatch makes it hard to audit, hard to test, and easy to drift if a sibling endpoint is added.

Fix: promote to constants.py:
- CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS = 60 * 60 (named for the unit)
- WEB_CSP_HEADER = 'default-src ...' (audit-friendly)

CSRF_COOKIE_NAME already at module level — keep there or also move.

Lens: hardcoded.
closed_reason: CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS + WEB_CSP_HEADER live in constants.py; web propose middleware/factory read from there

title: Promote CSRF cookie + CSP constants out of inline route code
Rename cli/_validate.validate_issue to validate_issue_record (collision)
description:
Two functions named validate_issue with different semantics:

- src/dogcat/models.py:383 validate_issue(issue: Issue) -> None (raises on bad object state)
- src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:88 validate_issue(record: dict, lineno: int) -> list[dict] (returns errors for a JSONL line)

Both imported by tests; a future module importing both collides.

Fix: rename cli/_validate.validate_issue → validate_issue_record. Mirrors validate_proposal_record already in same file at line 297. Symmetric and self-documenting.

Lens: naming.
closed_reason: Renamed _validate.validate_issue → validate_issue_record to mirror validate_proposal_record and avoid collision with models.validate_issue

title: Rename cli/_validate.validate_issue to validate_issue_record (collision)
Rename EventLog.emit to try_emit (lies about swallow contract)
description:
src/dogcat/event_log.py:101-139: EventLog.emit best-effort wrapper swallows OSError/RuntimeError at DEBUG. The name 'emit' suggests synchronous commitment; callers don't know it can silently fail.

Fix: rename to try_emit or emit_best_effort with a docstring spelling out the swallow contract. EventLog.append (the underlying file write) keeps its name.

Lens: naming + error-gaps.
closed_reason: EventLog.emit renamed to try_emit; emit kept as a back-compat alias and storage/inbox callers migrated

title: Rename EventLog.emit to try_emit (lies about swallow contract)
Replace inline _field_value reimplementation with _diff.field_value import
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_diff.py:145-149 reimplements field_value inline (5 lines). Three other modules (storage.py, _validate.py, plus _diff.py itself) all delegate or reimplement.

_diff.field_value is the canonical home (storage.py:15 already imports it).

Fix: replace cli/_cmd_diff.py inline copy with from dogcat._diff import field_value. Optionally drop the thin delegators in storage.py:437-440 and cli/_validate.py:436-440 (they add no value).

Lens: duplicates + simplify-code.
closed_reason: _cmd_diff._field_value is now a re-export of dogcat._diff.field_value (third inline copy removed)

title: Replace inline _field_value reimplementation with _diff.field_value import
Web POST mutates app.state.namespaces before write succeeds
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:241: valid_namespaces.append(namespace) is called BEFORE the asyncio.to_thread(_create_proposal_sync, ...) write. If the write fails, the namespace is still in valid_namespaces for the rest of the process lifetime. Subsequent submissions to that namespace bypass the 'is_new_namespace' branch (they appear to exist) and won't be persisted to pinned_namespaces.

Fix: append only after successful _create_proposal_sync (move next to the existing _persist_pinned_namespace call).

Lens: error-gaps.
closed_reason: Web propose POST appends to valid_namespaces only after a successful create, so a failed write doesn't poison subsequent submits

title: Web POST mutates app.state.namespaces before write succeeds
Type apply_common_filters as list[Issue] (not list[Any])
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:349-436 (apply_common_filters), :614,635,652,686 (proposal helpers): all type their lists as list[Any] and storage as Any. Function bodies assume the full Issue / Proposal shape (issue_type.value, priority, labels, owner, parent, metadata, comments, full_id, namespace).

list[Any] defeats pyright strict mode. ~25 callers across cli/_cmd_*.py.

Fix: type as list[Issue] / list[Proposal] and JSONLStorage | None (forward-ref to avoid circular imports).

Lens: type-structs.
closed_reason: apply_common_filters + apply_comment_filter typed list[Issue]; load_open_inbox_proposals + load_remote_inbox_proposals typed list[Proposal]; storage typed JSONLStorage|None

title: Type apply_common_filters as list[Issue] (not list[Any])

description:
src/dogcat/storage.py has the 4-line block:

    resolved_id = self.resolve_id(issue_id)
    if resolved_id is None:
        msg = f'Issue {issue_id} not found'
        raise ValueError(msg)

repeated ~19 times: lines 1145, 1150, 1197, 1202, 1238, 1256, 1283, 1288, 1324, 1329, 1361, 1379, 1397 plus several earlier methods (close, reopen, delete).

Add JSONLStorage._resolve_or_raise(self, issue_id, *, label='Issue') -> str. Replaces ~95 lines with single-line calls. Mirror in InboxStorage for proposal lookups (close, delete, get).

Lens: duplicates.
closed_reason: JSONLStorage._resolve_or_raise + InboxStorage._resolve_or_raise replace ~14 inline 'resolve or raise ValueError' blocks

title: Add JSONLStorage._resolve_or_raise helper (19 inline copies)

description:
Pattern repeats in cli/_cmd_create.py:313-338 (×4: depends_on, blocks, duplicate_of, parent) and cli/_cmd_update.py:243-260 (×2: duplicate_of, parent):

if foo:

@dataclass(frozen=True)

    level: Literal['error', 'warning']
    message: str

Bump signatures: list[dict[str, str]] → list[ValidationError]. Doctor JSON output stays identical via dataclasses.asdict.

Pyright catches typo'd e['levle']; Literal rules out third-state bugs.

Lens: type-structs.
closed_reason: ValidationError dataclass added with to_dict() for the legacy {level, message} shape; full callsite migration deferred to incremental work

description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:96-230 — _load is 135 lines, 6 levels deep. Multi-record-type dispatch (link/dependency/event/issue) × add/remove ops, plus version tracking + last-line tolerance + warning emission. The if rtype == 'link' and elif rtype == 'dependency' blocks are nearly identical structurally.

Extract:
- _parse_link_record(data, link_map) -> None
- _parse_dependency_record(data, dep_map) -> None
- _parse_issue_record(data, issues) -> None

Hot loop becomes a dispatch table: parsers = {'link': _parse_link_record, ...}. Function shrinks to ~50 lines.

Lens: complexity.

description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_create.py:71-396 — _create_impl is ~190 lines of body with 14+ branches: shorthand-vs-flag conflict (×3), title required, body/description merge, label parsing, priority resolution (3-way), type resolution (3-way), status resolution (3-way), dependency target validation (×2), duplicate_of resolution, parent resolution, then create + post-create dependency adds.

Body shrinks to ~80 lines.

Linked working tree scenarios: shared .dogcats across multiple checkouts
description:
Add tests covering linked-working-tree scenarios (the 'git wt' feature). Linked working trees share a single .git directory (and therefore a single .dogcats/issues.jsonl, since it lives in the working tree of the primary checkout) but each linked tree has its own HEAD and index. Need to verify behavior when:

- Two linked trees are on different branches and both modify .dogcats/issues.jsonl, then one branch is merged into the other
- A linked tree is created from a branch with pending dcat changes
- dcat commands run in a secondary linked tree (does it find .dogcats correctly via git rev-parse, or does it walk up and miss it?)
- Compaction/migration runs in one linked tree while another is active
- The merge driver fires on a merge performed inside a secondary linked tree

Note: project policy is not to use this feature ourselves, but external users may. Goal is to confirm dcat does not silently corrupt state in such setups, or to document the limitation if it does.

title.new: Linked working tree scenarios: shared .dogcats across multiple checkouts

GitHub server-side merge strategies: squash, rebase, update branch
description:
GitHub's squash-merge, rebase-merge, and 'Update branch' features perform merges on GitHub's servers without invoking the local merge driver registered in .gitattributes. The result lands as a regular commit on the target branch with whatever GitHub's strategy produced. For .dogcats/issues.jsonl this means the append-only log can be collapsed (squash) or rewritten (rebase) without our reconciliation logic running.

Need tests that simulate each strategy locally (replaying what GitHub does server-side) and verify the resulting state — either it works correctly, or it is detected and recovered by dcat doctor. This task may reveal that squash/rebase merges via GitHub are unsafe for our format, in which case the deliverable is documentation of the limitation and a post-merge recovery path.

title.new: GitHub server-side merge strategies: squash, rebase, update branch

Shallow, partial, and sparse clone test coverage
description:
CI pipelines commonly use 'git clone --depth=1', partial clones ('--filter=blob:none'), or sparse-checkout. The merge driver may rely on merge-base history that has been truncated in shallow clones, or on .dogcats files that are excluded by sparse-checkout.

Need tests that exercise: depth-1 clones running dcat commands, depth-1 clones performing a merge that triggers our driver, partial clones, and sparse-checkout configurations that exclude .dogcats. Goal: confirm dcat either works or fails clearly with actionable messages.

Fresh clone without merge driver registered: detection and UX
description:
.gitattributes declares the merge driver, but 'git config merge.dogcat.driver' must be set per-clone (typically by running dcat doctor or an install command). New contributors who clone the repo and immediately perform a merge before running dcat will not get the driver invoked — git will fall back to the default text merge, which will likely corrupt .dogcats/issues.jsonl in subtle ways.

Need tests that simulate a fresh clone with the driver not yet configured, and verify either: (a) dcat doctor detects the missing driver config and provides a clear fix, or (b) a first-run command auto-installs it. Document the failure mode if it currently silently corrupts data.

Revert of a merge commit: 'git revert -m 1' test coverage
description:
Reverting a merge commit ('git revert -m 1 <merge-sha>') is a notoriously tricky operation for structured-data merges. The revert applies the inverse of the merge against one of the parents, which for our append-only JSONL log may produce nonsensical state (deleting events that were correctly reconciled).

Need tests covering: revert of a clean merge, revert of a merge that involved conflicts, revert-of-revert (the 're-merge' problem from the git docs), and verification that dcat doctor flags any inconsistent state produced.

git bisect test coverage: detached HEAD and rapid checkouts
description:
'git bisect' performs many rapid checkouts in detached-HEAD state. If any dcat command runs during bisect (via hooks, a developer's habit of running 'dcat list', or CI scripts that invoke dcat), it must tolerate the detached state without writing back stale data or crashing.

Need tests that exercise dcat commands during a bisect session, verifying read-only commands work correctly and write commands either succeed sensibly or refuse with a clear message.

description:

Force-push after rebase: collaborator pull-rebase recovery
description:
When a developer rebases and force-pushes a shared branch ('--force-with-lease'), collaborators pulling that branch encounter a non-fast-forward situation. Their local .dogcats may have new events appended on top of the now-rewritten history.

Need tests simulating: contributor A rebases and force-pushes; contributor B has appended local changes to the old tip, then runs 'git pull --rebase'. Verify the merge driver / dcat reconciliation produces a coherent log without losing events from either side. Also cover the case where contributor B has not yet pulled and runs 'git fetch' followed by manual reset to the rewritten branch.

Adversarial fuzz audit findings (20-fuzzer review)
description:
Consolidated findings from a 20-agent adversarial code review covering: empty-inputs, boundary-values, type-confusion, unicode-chaos, concurrency, path-traversal, injection, state-machine, resource-exhaustion, time-travel, permission-escalation, malformed-input, network-failure, config-chaos, dependency-failure, locale-chaos, filesystem-edge, api-abuse, upgrade-path, adversarial-user.

Tier breakdown: 19 Red (exploitable now), 6 Orange (missing use cases), 12 Yellow (should harden), 1 Green (consider). Each child issue cites exact file:line, the attack vector, the impact, and the proposed fix. Most fixes are localized — isinstance guards, missing validators called at the storage boundary, status-finality on issue merge, body-size middleware, and lock-acquisition fixes.

Recurring systemic gaps the children point at:
- Input validation lives only at the web boundary; the storage layer trusts callers, so every non-web write path bypasses MAX_TITLE_LEN / is_valid_namespace / control-character stripping.
- JSONL readers (storage, inbox, event_log, merge_driver) catch (JSONDecodeError, ValueError, KeyError) but not AttributeError/TypeError, so a non-dict line bricks the database.
- 'Tolerate corrupt last line' is asymmetric — mid-file errors abort the whole load with no in-tool repair.
- Merge driver compares ISO timestamps as strings and applies plain LWW to issues (proposals already have status-finality).
acceptance: Each child issue closed or explicitly deferred. The 'Red' children should land before the next release.

title.new: Adversarial fuzz audit findings (20-fuzzer review)

resolve_partial_id("") matches every issue ID
description:
src/dogcat/_id_resolve.py:50-54 builds matches via 'full_id.endswith(partial_id)'. For partial_id='', endswith returns True for every full id. With one matching issue in the database the resolver returns that issue.

Trigger: any CLI command whose first positional arg is an id and the script forgot to validate it. dcat close "", dcat update "", dcat delete "", dcat inbox close "", dcat comment "" add --text x, dcat dep "" add --depends-on x, dcat link "" add --related x — all silently mutate the only matching issue/proposal.

Impact: silent destructive mutation. The lone-issue case has no ambiguity to detect.

Fix: reject 'not partial_id' at the top of resolve_partial_id.
acceptance: resolve_partial_id('') returns None (or raises) without scanning the id set; tests cover empty/whitespace partial IDs across issues and proposals.

Non-dict JSONL line crashes _load with uncaught AttributeError/TypeError
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:155-186 (and src/dogcat/inbox.py:87-117) catch (orjson.JSONDecodeError, ValueError, KeyError). A line whose JSON decodes to null/42/[]/'string' returns a non-dict; the next data.get(...) raises AttributeError, which is NOT in the except. The 'tolerate corrupt last line' branch lives behind the same except, so even last-line non-dict lines bypass tolerance.

Mid-file occurrence aborts every dcat invocation that constructs storage (list, show, update, close, doctor, archive, web, TUI). Recovery requires hand-editing issues.jsonl which CLAUDE.md forbids.

Same pattern in src/dogcat/stream.py incremental parser, src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_archive.py raw-line iteration, src/dogcat/merge_driver.py.

Fix: add (AttributeError, TypeError) to the except tuples; or 'if not isinstance(data, dict): continue' after orjson.loads. _validate.parse_raw_records already does the latter — copy it.
acceptance: A non-dict JSONL line anywhere in issues.jsonl/inbox.jsonl logs a warning and is skipped; CLI commands continue to function; regression tests cover null, list, scalar at first/middle/last positions.

acceptance.new: A non-dict JSONL line anywhere in issues.jsonl/inbox.jsonl logs a warning and is skipped; CLI commands continue to function; regression tests cover null, list, scalar at first/middle/last positions.

Mid-file malformed JSONL line bricks the CLI
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:174-185 only forgives the literal last non-blank line via 'is_last_line'. Any other malformed line — truncated record from an editor crash, partial UTF-8 from an interrupted append, BOM at the head, future enum value — re-raises ValueError and breaks every subsequent dcat invocation. No in-tool repair; CLAUDE.md forbids hand-editing.

Same pattern in src/dogcat/inbox.py.

Fix: switch to skip-and-warn for any malformed line (matching _validate.parse_raw_records behavior); set self._needs_compaction = True so the next mutation rewrites cleanly. Add a 'dcat admin repair-jsonl' that copies bad lines into a .bad sidecar.
acceptance: A malformed mid-file JSONL line is logged as a warning and skipped; subsequent dcat calls succeed; doctor surfaces the count of bad lines; new admin subcommand exists for explicit repair.

title.new: Mid-file malformed JSONL line bricks the CLI

Future enum values crash older readers (forward-compat hole)
description:
src/dogcat/models.py:581,583,601 calls Status(raw_status) / IssueType(raw) / IssueType(raw_original_type). src/dogcat/inbox.py:96-100 calls ProposalStatus(...). src/dogcat/storage.py:250 calls DependencyType(...). All raise ValueError on unknown values.

The schema docstring promises older readers ignore unknown fields. Unknown enum VALUES are not handled the same way: a future status='snoozed' or issue_type='checkpoint' record sandwiched in mid-file aborts the load. SCHEMA_BREAKING_THRESHOLD is None so even bumps don't trigger the warning.

Fix: wrap enum constructors in try/except, fall back to a sentinel + log; treat unknown enum values as unknown fields. Bump SCHEMA_BREAKING_THRESHOLD when adding new enum values.
acceptance: Forward-compat test: a record with a hypothetical future Status / IssueType / DependencyType / ProposalStatus value loads with a warning and the rest of the file parses cleanly.

title.new: Future enum values crash older readers (forward-compat hole)

CLI bypasses every input-length and namespace rule the web form enforces
description:
MAX_TITLE_LEN, MAX_DESC_LEN, MAX_NAMESPACE_LEN, NAMESPACE_PATTERN, is_valid_namespace, _normalize_namespace are wired only into src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py. Every CLI surface skips them: dcat propose, dcat create/c/new, dcat update --title/--description/--namespace, dcat comment, dcat rename-namespace, dcat config set namespace.

A 200 MB title or namespace='\x1b[31mevil\x1b[0m' or namespace='' written via CLI is persisted. Subsequent web boot loads the bloated/corrupt record on next _load. Six fuzzers flagged this from different angles (boundary-values, type-confusion, unicode-chaos, injection, path-traversal, locale-chaos).

Fix: enforce length, character class, and namespace pattern at the storage boundary in src/dogcat/models.py validate_issue/validate_proposal — so the constants in dogcat.constants become invariants instead of advisories. Call validate_issue from JSONLStorage.create AND update; same for inbox.
acceptance: validate_issue and validate_proposal enforce title/description/namespace caps and the namespace pattern; create/update/change_namespace paths fail loudly on violations; web and CLI surface identical rejection messages; tests cover oversized title, control-byte namespace, and CLI propose with the same rule the web form already enforces.

Every CLI surface skips them: dcat propose, dcat create/c/new, dcat update --title/--description/--namespace, dcat comment, dcat rename-namespace, dcat config set namespace.

A 200 MB title or namespace='\x1b[31mevil\x1b[0m' or namespace='' written via CLI is persisted. Subsequent web boot loads the bloated/corrupt record on next _load. Six fuzzers flagged this from different angles (boundary-values, type-confusion, unicode-chaos, injection, path-traversal, locale-chaos).

Terminal escape injection via web-submitted titles
description:
title/description/notes/labels/comments/owner accept arbitrary control bytes; orjson round-trips \x1b verbatim; every CLI display path (src/dogcat/cli/_formatting.py, src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py _format_proposal_*, format_event, post-create echoes in _cmd_create/_cmd_update/_cmd_close) emits raw bytes through typer.echo.

Attack: web POST title='\x1b[2J\x1b[H\x1b[31mFAKE PROMPT'. Maintainer running 'dcat inbox list' has terminal cleared and overwritten — including a forged shell prompt. For agents-in-the-loop the impact compounds: an agent piping inbox titles into another LLM context is prompt-injected.

Fix: strip C0/C1 control bytes (keep \t, \n) at write time in validate_issue/validate_proposal AND at render time as defense-in-depth. The single fix protects both terminals and agent pipelines.
acceptance: validate_issue/validate_proposal strip control bytes from string fields; render-time helper escapes any residual control bytes; regression tests cover \x1b[2J in title rendered via dcat inbox list, dcat read, dcat history.

title.new: Terminal escape injection via web-submitted titles

description.new:
title/description/notes/labels/comments/owner accept arbitrary control bytes; orjson round-trips \x1b verbatim; every CLI display path (src/dogcat/cli/_formatting.py, src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_inbox.py _format_proposal_*, format_event, post-create echoes in _cmd_create/_cmd_update/_cmd_close) emits raw bytes through typer.echo.

Attack: web POST title='\x1b[2J\x1b[H\x1b[31mFAKE PROMPT'. Maintainer running 'dcat inbox list' has terminal cleared and overwritten — including a forged shell prompt. For agents-in-the-loop the impact compounds: an agent piping inbox titles into another LLM context is prompt-injected.

Fix: strip C0/C1 control bytes (keep \t, \n) at write time in validate_issue/validate_proposal AND at render time as defense-in-depth. The single fix protects both terminals and agent pipelines.

acceptance.new: validate_issue/validate_proposal strip control bytes from string fields; render-time helper escapes any residual control bytes; regression tests cover \x1b[2J in title rendered via dcat inbox list, dcat read, dcat history.

title: Terminal escape injection via web-submitted titles
dcat archive rewrites issues.jsonl without holding storage lock
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_archive.py:383-492 reads, partitions into archived_lines/remaining_lines, writes a tempfile, and tmp.replace(target) — all without acquiring storage._file_lock(). flock on .issues.lock is the only thing serializing writes; archive ignores it entirely.

Same hole on inbox.jsonl in _archive_inbox at lines 67-128.

Sequence (data loss): T0 archive opens issues.jsonl for reading; T1 'dcat new' (other shell) acquires lock, appends, releases; T2 archive's tmp_main_path.replace(storage.path) fires. The brand-new issue is silently overwritten.

Fix: wrap the rewrite in storage._file_lock() (and the inbox path in inbox._file_lock()). Re-load under the lock before partitioning.
acceptance: Concurrent dcat new during dcat archive does not lose the new record; integration test exercises the race; locking ordering documented.

_save(_reload=False) rewrites stale memory, clobbering concurrent appends
description:
change_namespace (storage.py:1042), rename_namespace (1131), prune_tombstones (1697,1732), remove_dependencies — and inbox equivalents — load at construction, mutate in-memory, then _save_locked without re-reading. Lock acquired AFTER the rewrite plan is fixed.

A concurrent process can _append between our _load and our _save_locked. _save_locked's event-preservation loop only re-reads record_type='event' lines; fresh issue/proposal records are silently dropped on rewrite.

Worst with web server (long-lived app.state.inbox) — inbox.rename_namespace silently nukes proposals submitted via the form during the rename.

Fix: switch to _reload=True for inter-process callers. Or compare on-disk size before rewrite and re-load if it grew.
acceptance: Inter-process appends are not lost across rename_namespace / prune_tombstones / remove_dependencies; integration tests exercise the race for both storage and inbox.

acceptance.new: Inter-process appends are not lost across rename_namespace / prune_tombstones / remove_dependencies; integration tests exercise the race for both storage and inbox.

close() resurrects tombstones; update() permits tombstone -> open
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:1144-1185 (close) has no current-status guard. dcat delete dc-x then dcat close dc-x flips a tombstone back to status='closed' with both deleted_at AND closed_at set. Inconsistent with reopen() at line 1209 which DOES gate on Status.CLOSED.

src/dogcat/storage.py:899-983 (update) allows arbitrary status transitions. dcat update dc-x --status open after dcat delete dc-x re-opens the tombstoned issue but leaves deleted_at/deleted_by/deleted_reason populated.

delete() called twice on the same issue (lines 1236-1309) overwrites deleted_at/deleted_reason silently — forensic record loss.

Fix: gate close() and update() on current status (refuse from Status.TOMBSTONE), or clear deleted_* when transitioning away from TOMBSTONE. delete() should be idempotent when the issue is already tombstoned.
acceptance: close() raises on a tombstoned issue; update() rejects status changes from TOMBSTONE (or clears deleted_*); double-delete is idempotent; tests cover all three transitions.

title.new: close() resurrects tombstones; update() permits tombstone -> open

Issue merge driver lacks status finality; tombstones revert at merge
description:
src/dogcat/merge_driver.py:356-371 does plain LWW on updated_at strings for issues. Branch A 'dcat delete dc-x' at T=10:00 (tombstone, updated_at=10:00). Branch B 'dcat update dc-x --notes' at T=10:01 (still open, updated_at=10:01). Merge keeps B because 10:01 > 10:00 — tombstone is silently lost.

Proposals already use _PROPOSAL_STATUS_RANK at lines 262-266,372-400 — issues should follow the same pattern.

Combined with child dogcat-4g76 (update permits tombstone->open), an agent can write a later-timestamped open record on a feature branch and silently restore tombstones at merge.

Fix: rank issue statuses with tombstone > closed > * > draft; break ties on updated_at.
acceptance: Issue merge applies status-finality before updated_at fallback; concurrent edit on B cannot revert A's tombstone; merge-driver test covers the scenario.

Merge driver compares ISO timestamps lexicographically
description:
src/dogcat/merge_driver.py:367-369,397-400,457 do new_ts >= old_ts on raw ISO strings. Counterexample:
  '2026-04-25T10:00:00+05:00' (= 05:00 UTC) >= '2026-04-25T08:00:00+00:00' (= 08:00 UTC, 3h later)
  → True lexicographically, but B is later in absolute time.

Cross-timezone collaborators silently keep older edits. Same flaw in proposal LWW tiebreak and event sort. 'Z' vs '+00:00' for the same instant compare unequal lex.

Fix: parse to aware datetimes (or normalize to UTC strings) before compare.
acceptance: Merge picks the absolute-later updated_at across mixed-tz strings; tests cover Tokyo (+09) vs UTC vs PDT (-07) cases plus 'Z' vs '+00:00'.

acceptance.new: Merge picks the absolute-later updated_at across mixed-tz strings; tests cover Tokyo (+09) vs UTC vs PDT (-07) cases plus 'Z' vs '+00:00'.

title.new: Merge driver compares ISO timestamps lexicographically

.dogcatrc walk-up trusts any ancestor with no boundary or warning
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:256-300 (find_dogcats_dir) and src/dogcat/config.py:138 (_find_rc_parent) walk Path.cwd() upward to filesystem root, taking the first .dogcatrc / .dogcats found. parse_dogcatrc reads the path and target.resolve() follows symlinks. No ownership check, no boundary, no stderr warning when an out-of-tree rc is used.

Attack: anyone with write access to /tmp/.dogcatrc (multi-tenant host, shared mount, CI workspace) silently re-roots every dcat command in that subtree to attacker-controlled storage. Issues created by the victim land in attacker's tree, attributed to victim's git email.

Same gap for src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:709-720 _find_claude_dir which writes settings.{local,}.json.

Fix: bound the walk to git toplevel or $HOME; warn on stderr when an out-of-tree rc is used; refuse rc files whose target is owned by a different user than the rc owner.
acceptance: Walk-up stops at git toplevel by default; opt-in via env var to extend; out-of-tree rc emits a warning; refuses target with foreign ownership unless --unsafe-cross-user is set.

CSRF token never rotates; captured pair replayable for 1h
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:218-308 reads cookie, hmac.compare_digest with form field. Line 242 explicitly does NOT rotate ('session_token = cookie_token' with comment about multi-tab usability). CSRF_COOKIE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS = 3600.

A captured (cookie, form-token) pair stays valid for an hour, replayable from any HTTP client (CSRF protects browsers, not direct attackers). Combined with --allow-creating-namespaces, an attacker can grow pinned_namespaces without bound during the window. No Origin/Referer check, no per-namespace nonce, no idempotency key. IDGenerator uses microsecond timestamps so each replay creates a new proposal.

Adjacent gap: same captured cookie (any client-controlled bytes) is accepted as the token with no entropy/shape check.

Fix: bind tokens to a server-issued nonce table (TTL ~10 min, consumed on POST); validate Origin/Sec-Fetch-Site against an allowlist; cap pinned_namespaces count; validate cookie shape ([A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}) before reuse.
acceptance: Tokens are single-use within a 10 min window; Origin allowlist enforced; --host 0.0.0.0 emits a warning; pinned_namespaces growth capped.

title.new: CSRF token never rotates; captured pair replayable for 1h

No body-size cap before Form() parsing — memory blow-up DoS
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:206-212 declares Form(...) fields with default Starlette / python-multipart settings. MAX_TITLE_LEN=500 and MAX_DESC_LEN=50_000 are checked AFTER python-multipart materializes the body. A malicious Content-Length: 10737418240 (10 GB) of urlencoded description=AAAA... is buffered fully before route logic runs.

src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:131-141 adds only SecurityHeadersMiddleware; no body-size middleware, no proxy in front. A single attacker request can OOM the server. Combined with --host 0.0.0.0 it's a LAN DoS independent of any rate limiter (dogcat-4407 is per-frequency).

Fix: add a body-size middleware (~256 KB) before form parsing.
acceptance: Middleware rejects bodies above ~256 KB (configurable) before form parsing; integration test exercises a 1 GB request and confirms 413 Payload Too Large without buffering.

acceptance.new: Middleware rejects bodies above ~256 KB (configurable) before form parsing; integration test exercises a 1 GB request and confirms 413 Payload Too Large without buffering.

Reflected namespace on error path → 2x bandwidth + CPU amplification
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:265-269 normalizes via NFKC and validates length AFTER reflecting the unbounded user-supplied namespace into the error template. Verified: ns_size=5_000_000 yields resp_len=10_003_680 (2x amplification). NFKC over a 5 MB payload also costs CPU.

Other rejection paths use fixed messages — only namespace reflects user input.

Fix: pre-cap namespace length before _normalize_namespace (e.g. > MAX_NAMESPACE_LEN * 4); have _render_error substitute a placeholder when the namespace is over-length.
acceptance: Server-side error response is bounded regardless of submitted namespace size; integration test confirms a 5 MB namespace request yields a small error response without NFKC over the full payload.

title.new: Reflected namespace on error path → 2x bandwidth + CPU amplification

acceptance.new: Server-side error response is bounded regardless of submitted namespace size; integration test confirms a 5 MB namespace request yields a small error response without NFKC over the full payload.

JSONLStorage.update accepts arbitrary types via setattr
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:899-983 walks UPDATABLE_FIELDS and does setattr(issue, key, value) with no isinstance check on most fields. update(id, {'metadata': 'hello'}) reaches the setattr, then crashes inside diff_metadata's set(new) — issue is mutated, event record build raises afterward, half-applied write.

Other gaps: priority=True passes validate_priority because isinstance(True, int) is True (bool is int subclass) and stores boolean in priority field. status=42 reaches setattr because the isinstance(value, str) guard only converts strings. labels='bug' produces character-set membership (set('bug')={'b','u','g'}) silently breaking --label filters.

Fix: isinstance-check each updatable field; call validate_issue after the mutation as defense-in-depth.
acceptance: JSONLStorage.update raises TypeError for non-string status, non-int priority, non-list labels, non-dict metadata; tests cover each field.

acceptance.new: JSONLStorage.update raises TypeError for non-string status, non-int priority, non-list labels, non-dict metadata; tests cover each field.

Recursive cycle detection blows stack on deep dependency chain
description:
src/dogcat/deps.py:166-189 detect_cycles uses dfs(neighbor, path[:]) — fresh O(N) slice per neighbor, O(V*E) time, O(V^2) frame memory. Default Python recursion limit is 1000.

src/dogcat/deps.py:237-248 would_create_cycle.can_reach also recurses. src/dogcat/cli/_validate.py:439-462 _detect_cycles._dfs same. _build_dep_map runs on every would_create_cycle call (O(D)).

Build a 1001-deep dep chain via repeated dcat dep add. The 1001st add_dependency triggers can_reach recursion → RecursionError. From that point every dcat dep add, dcat doctor, and dcat ready crashes.

Fix: convert all three DFSs to iterative with an explicit stack; cache _build_dep_map within a single CLI invocation.
acceptance: Cycle detection handles 10000-deep chains without RecursionError; tests cover the depth threshold.

title.new: Recursive cycle detection blows stack on deep dependency chain

acceptance.new: Cycle detection handles 10000-deep chains without RecursionError; tests cover the depth threshold.

EventLog.read crashes on any malformed line
description:
src/dogcat/event_log.py:215-249 has no try/except around orjson.loads(line). Any JSONDecodeError or non-dict line propagates a raw traceback to the caller. dcat history, dcat workflow, dcat admin all fall over.

Asymmetric with _load which tolerates a corrupt last line. Re-introduces the AttributeError leak via data.get('record_type') on a non-dict.

Fix: mirror _load's tolerance: try/except around the loop body that catches (JSONDecodeError, ValueError, KeyError, AttributeError, TypeError) and log+skip.
acceptance: EventLog.read survives malformed lines; dcat history runs on a file with a corrupt event line; tests cover non-dict + invalid-JSON cases.

_save_locked event-passthrough crashes on the same line _load skipped
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:362-394 (_save_locked event-preservation block) catches (orjson.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) but not AttributeError/TypeError. After _load tolerates a corrupt last line and sets _needs_compaction=True, the next mutation triggers _save which calls _save_locked → re-parses the same file → re-raises on the non-dict line.

Sequence: _load tolerates → _needs_compaction → next mutation → _save_locked crashes → user sees the same error every time. Self-perpetuating crash; the recovery path becomes the failure path.

Fix: match _load's exception tolerance in the event-preservation block (add AttributeError/TypeError + isinstance guard).
acceptance: Compaction succeeds when issues.jsonl has a non-dict line; the rewritten file omits the bad record; test covers _needs_compaction → mutation → save with a non-dict last line.

title.new: _save_locked event-passthrough crashes on the same line _load skipped

acceptance.new: Compaction succeeds when issues.jsonl has a non-dict line; the rewritten file omits the bad record; test covers _needs_compaction → mutation → save with a non-dict last line.

migrate_from_beads silently drops 10 fields per issue
description:
src/dogcat/migrate.py:75-135. Confirmed by grep — migrate_issue never references external_ref, design, acceptance, notes, parent, closed_by, updated_by, comments, closed_reason, metadata. dcat import-beads on a beads database with any of those fields populated produces issues with all of them empty. No warning, no count.

Adjacent gap at lines 16-34 (parse_datetime): malformed timestamp returns None, caller does 'parse_datetime(...) or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)' so issue inherits the migration time. Combined with idgen.generate_issue_id hashing 'title:timestamp.isoformat()', the same record imported twice gets different IDs.

Fix: thread the missing fields 1:1 by name (the beads schema uses identical names); raise on non-empty-but-unparseable timestamps and increment 'failed' rather than silently coercing.
acceptance: All 10 fields propagate end-to-end; malformed timestamps surface as failed imports with verbose detail; integration test imports a fixture covering every field.

acceptance.new: All 10 fields propagate end-to-end; malformed timestamps surface as failed imports with verbose detail; integration test imports a fixture covering every field.

dcat web propose only guards uvicorn; jinja2/python-multipart fail confusingly
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_web.py:109-117 wraps only 'import uvicorn' in try/except ImportError with the friendly install hint. 'from dogcat.web.propose import create_app' runs outside the guard, so a missing jinja2 leaks a raw ModuleNotFoundError. A missing python-multipart starts the server cleanly but every POST 500s with a runtime error.

The friendly hint applies only to the LEAST likely missing extra in a partial install.

Fix: probe all four extras at startup; surface the same friendly hint for any of jinja2/python-multipart/fastapi/uvicorn.

/openapi.json exposed despite docs disabled
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/__init__.py:92-96 sets docs_url=None and redoc_url=None but openapi_url defaults to /openapi.json. Verified live: GET /docs and /redoc return 404 but GET /openapi.json returns 200 with the full schema including form-field names and validation error shapes.

Impact: full route and schema disclosure on the bind address (LAN if --host 0.0.0.0).

Fix: pass openapi_url=None to the FastAPI(...) constructor.
acceptance: GET /openapi.json returns 404; integration test confirms.

parse_duration lowercases the input then passes to fromisoformat
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:484. raw = value.strip().lower() then datetime.fromisoformat(raw). Python fromisoformat only accepts uppercase 'Z'. dcat snooze ID 2026-04-25T00:00:00Z is rejected with 'Invalid duration' even though the help text claims ISO8601 support.

Adjacent gap at lines 503-510: naive ISO branch hard-codes today's tzinfo via datetime.now().astimezone().tzinfo. dcat snooze ID 2026-12-31 in CEST stamps +02:00 even though Europe/Oslo is +01:00 on that date — off by an hour across DST.

Fix: parse the relative regex on the lowered form, but feed the original-case value to fromisoformat. For naive ISO use a real zoneinfo zone instead of a fixed-offset snapshot.
acceptance: dcat snooze ID 2026-04-25T00:00:00Z is accepted; DST-crossing naive ISO inputs round-trip correctly.

src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:484. raw = value.strip().lower() then datetime.fromisoformat(raw). Python fromisoformat only accepts uppercase 'Z'. dcat snooze ID 2026-04-25T00:00:00Z is rejected with 'Invalid duration' even though the help text claims ISO8601 support.

Adjacent gap at lines 503-510: naive ISO branch hard-codes today's tzinfo via datetime.now().astimezone().tzinfo. dcat snooze ID 2026-12-31 in CEST stamps +02:00 even though Europe/Oslo is +01:00 on that date — off by an hour across DST.

Fix: parse the relative regex on the lowered form, but feed the original-case value to fromisoformat. For naive ISO use a real zoneinfo zone instead of a fixed-offset snapshot.

dcat doctor --post-merge silently no-ops outside repo or with external .dogcats
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:11899-11908. When repo_root_path is None (not in a git repo) or relative_to raises (.dogcats lives outside the git repo, common for shared trackers via .dogcatrc), the entire concurrent-edit detector is silently skipped via 'except ValueError: pass'.

User runs --post-merge expecting an audit, gets a clean exit and assumes nothing concurrent happened.

Fix: print 'post-merge skipped: no git repo / .dogcats outside repo' instead of silent pass.
acceptance: --post-merge prints a clear skip reason when it cannot run; tests cover both the no-repo and external-.dogcats cases.

Reflected /?submitted=true banner is fully attacker-controlled
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:181-202 reads submitted, id, title, namespace from query params and renders them into propose.html lines 21-25. Jinja2 autoescape blocks XSS, but the page renders an attacker-controlled 'success' banner inside dogcat's brand chrome with the real CSRF cookie attached. Convincing phishing surface.

submitted='true' is the only gate; no signature on (id, title); no length cap; no check that id exists in storage.

Fix: look up the proposal by id server-side (or HMAC-sign the redirect query and verify in the GET handler); change POST to redirect to /{id} and drop reflected text entirely.
acceptance: /?submitted=true&id=...&title=... no longer reflects attacker text; either the title is fetched from storage or the redirect is signed and tampered queries are rejected.

_is_default_branch matches localized git stderr → auto-compaction silently disabled
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:439-478. result.stderr.lower() looks for the literal English 'not a git repository'. Git emits localized strings under non-English LC_ALL (French 'ce n'est pas un dépôt git', German 'Kein Git-Repository', Japanese, Spanish, Russian, etc.) — substring check fails, _is_default_branch returns False, should_compact() AND _is_default_branch() is False forever, auto-compaction never runs.

Same risk in src/dogcat/git.py:21-50 _run helper for any future caller that parses stdout/stderr text.

Fix: pass env={**os.environ, 'LC_ALL': 'C', 'LANG': 'C'} to subprocess.run. Git always emits the C-locale strings under LC_ALL=C.
acceptance: Auto-compaction triggers correctly outside a git repo regardless of LC_ALL; tests cover French/German/Japanese locale stderr.

acceptance.new: Auto-compaction triggers correctly outside a git repo regardless of LC_ALL; tests cover French/German/Japanese locale stderr.

title.new: _is_default_branch matches localized git stderr → auto-compaction silently disabled

Same risk in src/dogcat/git.py:21-50 _run helper for any future caller that parses stdout/stderr text.

Fix: pass env={**os.environ, 'LC_ALL': 'C', 'LANG': 'C'} to subprocess.run. Git always emits the C-locale strings under LC_ALL=C.

Subprocess git calls have no timeout
description:
src/dogcat/git.py:36,43 (shared _run) and src/dogcat/storage.py:453 (inline subprocess.run for _is_default_branch) call subprocess.run without a timeout argument. Every git invocation blocks indefinitely on hangs.

Hang triggers: stalled NFS HOME (slow ~/.gitconfig read), dead credential helper daemon, LFS smudge with broken LFS server, stalled git-config readlink. _is_default_branch is called after every locked append crossing the compaction threshold so it's the most exposed.

Locks (src/dogcat/locking.py:78) release in finally so SIGINT works, but SIGKILL leaves a stale lock until the 30s timeout.

Fix: add timeout=10 (env-driven via DCAT_GIT_TIMEOUT_SECS) in git._run; catch subprocess.TimeoutExpired.
acceptance: Stalled git invocations time out within 10s and surface a clear error; env-var override works; SIGTERM-induced stale locks documented.

title.new: Subprocess git calls have no timeout

acceptance.new: Stalled git invocations time out within 10s and surface a clear error; env-var override works; SIGTERM-induced stale locks documented.

Hang triggers: stalled NFS HOME (slow ~/.gitconfig read), dead credential helper daemon, LFS smudge with broken LFS server, stalled git-config readlink. _is_default_branch is called after every locked append crossing the compaction threshold so it's the most exposed.

Locks (src/dogcat/locking.py:78) release in finally so SIGINT works, but SIGKILL leaves a stale lock until the 30s timeout.

Fix: add timeout=10 (env-driven via DCAT_GIT_TIMEOUT_SECS) in git._run; catch subprocess.TimeoutExpired.

save_config / save_local_config are not atomic
description:
src/dogcat/config.py:4934-4936,4949-4952 directly truncate-then-write:

  with config_path.open('wb') as f:
      tomli_w.dump(config, f)

ENOSPC / kill / power-loss mid-write leaves a truncated TOML. _load_toml at lines 4794-4801 swallows TOMLDecodeError and returns {} → all configured settings (namespace, visible_namespaces, git_tracking, allow_creating_namespaces) silently lost. Next dcat config set clobbers the broken file before the user notices.

Compare _atomic_write_json in _cmd_doctor.py:12118 (write-temp + fsync + replace) — same pattern is missing here.

Fix: route through write-temp + fsync + replace. Adjacent: _persist_pinned_namespace race noted in dogcat-4qpy children.
acceptance: save_config and save_local_config use atomic rewrite; integration test simulates mid-write crash and confirms no truncation.

Atomic rewrite silently demotes 0644 to 0600
description:
src/dogcat/_jsonl_io.py:37-58. tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile defaults to mode 0600; tmp_path.replace(target) keeps that mode. Verified on macOS: chmod 644 issues.jsonl, run a compaction, stat shows 600.

Impact: shared .dogcats/ (multi-user dev box, CI service writing for others to read) becomes inaccessible to everyone except the writer after the first compaction/archive/prune. With core.fileMode=true, git diff shows a permission-only change polluting subsequent commits.

Fix: before tmp_path.replace, mirror the original target mode: os.chmod(tmp_path, target.stat().st_mode & 0o7777) when target exists.
acceptance: atomic_rewrite_jsonl preserves the target's existing mode; tests cover 0644 and 0664 starting modes.

title.new: Atomic rewrite silently demotes 0644 to 0600

acceptance.new: atomic_rewrite_jsonl preserves the target's existing mode; tests cover 0644 and 0664 starting modes.

Whole CLI fails to load if any one _cmd_* import breaks
description:
src/dogcat/cli/__init__.py:7628-7693 imports every _cmd_* module eagerly and registers each into the Typer app. A broken transitive (rich ABI mismatch, watchdog wheel for wrong Python ABI, textual incompatible) collapses the whole CLI — including dcat doctor, the very tool users would reach for to diagnose.

Compounded by other eager imports: src/dogcat/config.py:4708 'import tomli_w' at module top (only needed by writers), src/dogcat/locking.py:4130 unconditional 'import fcntl' (Unix-only), src/dogcat/storage.py and others import dogcat._version unconditionally (gitignored, only exists post-build).

Fix: try/except around per-module register() in cli/__init__.py — log the failure with module name and continue. Lazy-import tomli_w in writers. Defensive import for _version with a fallback. Document Windows-unsupported in pyproject classifiers.
acceptance: A broken _cmd_* module produces a one-line warning and the rest of the CLI works; doctor still runs; test simulates import failure for one command.

Compounded by other eager imports: src/dogcat/config.py:4708 'import tomli_w' at module top (only needed by writers), src/dogcat/locking.py:4130 unconditional 'import fcntl' (Unix-only), src/dogcat/storage.py and others import dogcat._version unconditionally (gitignored, only exists post-build).

_persist_pinned_namespace race + non-atomic save_local_config
description:
src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:59-74 reads pinned_namespaces, appends, writes back. save_local_config (src/dogcat/config.py:4949-4952) doesn't lock and doesn't write atomically. Two concurrent submissions creating distinct new namespaces both read the old list, both append their own, last writer wins → one namespace lost. With concurrent contention the writes can interleave bytes mid-file → corrupt TOML.

Compounded by _inbox_stat being a plain dict shared across requests (lines 7358-7412): two threads stat()'d concurrently both reload, racing inbox._proposals.clear() with another concurrent inbox.create_proposal() → partial set returned to get_proposal_ids() → potential ID collision.

Fix: serialize pinned_namespaces writes with a lock; atomic save_local_config (already covered by sibling dogcat-issue for save_config); audit reload-on-stat for race windows.
acceptance: Concurrent web POSTs creating new namespaces both succeed and both names persist in pinned_namespaces; load test confirms no TOML corruption.

title.new: _persist_pinned_namespace race + non-atomic save_local_config

acceptance.new: Concurrent web POSTs creating new namespaces both succeed and both names persist in pinned_namespaces; load test confirms no TOML corruption.

DCAT_LOCK_TIMEOUT_SECS=inf produces unbounded lock wait
description:
src/dogcat/locking.py:24-33. float('inf') > 0 is True → timeout=inf → deadline = time.monotonic() + inf = inf → time.monotonic() >= deadline is forever False. Stale-lock-holder remediation that the docstring at lines 67-74 advertises is silently disabled.

NaN handled correctly (nan > 0 is False). Negatives handled correctly. inf and +Infinity slip through.

Fix: reject non-finite values via math.isfinite(value). Inline doc note about the env var being clamped.
acceptance: DCAT_LOCK_TIMEOUT_SECS=inf falls back to the default (30s); tests cover inf, -inf, nan, negative, zero, finite positive.

init.defaultBranch lets a collaborator trigger compaction on a feature branch
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:484-498 unions init.defaultBranch from per-repo git config into the known-default-branches set. init.defaultBranch is writable by anyone with .git/config access (a collaborator merging their branch can carry it). Setting init.defaultBranch=feature-foo on a feature branch causes dcat to auto-compact while on that branch — the operation is logically a no-op but produces noisy, conflict-prone diffs against main.

Fix: keep the union (legitimate use case for projects on develop/trunk) but log a one-line warning when a non-conventional branch name is used; consider promoting the override to project config (config.toml) where review is more visible.
acceptance: Compaction on a non-conventional default branch emits a stderr warning; documented in dcat doctor output.

List-typed config keys as strings iterate per-character; rename-namespace crashes
description:
TOML scalar-where-list is unguarded across the config layer:

- src/dogcat/config.py:459-478: visible_namespaces='frontend' makes set('frontend')={'f','r','o','n','t','e','d'} silently restricting visibility to single letters. Same for hidden_namespaces.
- src/dogcat/storage.py:1795: pinned_namespaces='foo' iterates 'f','o','o' as separate empty namespaces in the dashboard.
- src/dogcat/web/propose/routes.py:69 _persist_pinned_namespace: list(config.get('pinned_namespaces', [])) on a string yields ['f','o','o'], appends a real namespace, then save_local_config writes the corrupted char list back to TOML.
- src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_rename_namespace.py:88-91: 'old in pinned' on a string is substring check; pinned[pinned.index(old_namespace)] = new_namespace raises TypeError ('str' object does not support item assignment).
- src/dogcat/config.py:307-330 get_issue_prefix returns config['namespace'] as-is; namespace=1 (int) flows into f'{namespace}-{hash}' → ID corruption.

Fix: type-check each list-typed config key at the load_config boundary (or in get_namespaces / get_namespace_filter / get_issue_prefix). Coerce or reject on mismatch.
acceptance: TOML scalar-where-list values are rejected with a clear error rather than silently iterating; doctor surfaces such mismatches; tests cover string-where-list, int-where-string, list-where-string for namespace/visible_namespaces/hidden_namespaces/pinned_namespaces/inbox_remote.

description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_web.py:94: resolved_allow = bool(config.get('allow_creating_namespaces', False)). bool('false') / bool('no') / bool('0') / bool('off') all return True (non-empty strings are truthy in Python). A user editing config.toml directly (not via 'dcat config set' which coerces) writes allow_creating_namespaces='false' and gets the OPPOSITE of intended behavior on a security-sensitive toggle.

Compare git_tracking at _cmd_docs.py:233 and :1051-1053 which use 'is False' / 'is not False' identity checks — robust to strings.

Fix: match the 'is False' idiom; or coerce via _KNOWN_KEYS parsing at load time so string-where-bool is rejected.
acceptance: allow_creating_namespaces='false' / 'no' / '0' is rejected (or coerced to False) at load time; doctor reports the mismatch; test confirms the web endpoint refuses new namespaces.

parse_dogcatrc raises uncaught PermissionError at every CLI startup
description:
src/dogcat/config.py:54: content = rc_path.read_text().strip(). src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:8576-8580 only catches ValueError. PermissionError is OSError (NOT a ValueError subclass), so chmod 0 .dogcatrc / NFS hiccup / hardened CI all crash with a raw traceback.

Adjacent: parse_dogcatrc only does .strip() — preserves embedded \n/\r/\0 in path, so corrupt rc files become Path('/foo\n/bar') with confusing downstream errors. dcat init --dir / --use-existing also accept newlines and write them verbatim.

Fix: broaden the except to (ValueError, OSError); reject \n/\r/\0 in rc content (use splitlines()[0]); validate dcat init --dir / --use-existing reject control bytes.
acceptance: Unreadable / control-byte-laden .dogcatrc surfaces a clear error, not a traceback; tests cover chmod 0, embedded newline, NFS-mock failure.

title.new: parse_dogcatrc raises uncaught PermissionError at every CLI startup

acceptance.new: Unreadable / control-byte-laden .dogcatrc surfaces a clear error, not a traceback; tests cover chmod 0, embedded newline, NFS-mock failure.

description.new:
src/dogcat/config.py:54: content = rc_path.read_text().strip(). src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:8576-8580 only catches ValueError. PermissionError is OSError (NOT a ValueError subclass), so chmod 0 .dogcatrc / NFS hiccup / hardened CI all crash with a raw traceback.

Adjacent: parse_dogcatrc only does .strip() — preserves embedded \n/\r/\0 in path, so corrupt rc files become Path('/foo\n/bar') with confusing downstream errors. dcat init --dir / --use-existing also accept newlines and write them verbatim.

Fix: broaden the except to (ValueError, OSError); reject \n/\r/\0 in rc content (use splitlines()[0]); validate dcat init --dir / --use-existing reject control bytes.

title: parse_dogcatrc raises uncaught PermissionError at every CLI startup
Doctor crashes on non-dict settings.json
description:
src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_doctor.py:722-776 (_check_precompact_hook, _install_precompact_hook, _upgrade_precompact_hook): orjson.loads(settings_path.read_bytes()) returns whatever the user wrote. If the user has settings.json containing [], null, or '...', data.get('hooks', ...) raises AttributeError. The except catches only (OSError, orjson.JSONDecodeError).

Doctor IS the recovery tool — crashing it on a non-dict settings.json is the worst time.

Fix: 'if not isinstance(data, dict): continue' at the start of each loop.
acceptance: Doctor handles non-dict settings.json gracefully (skips that path with a warning); tests cover [], null, scalar.

Snooze duration overflow + far-future date + month semantics
description:
Three small UX papercuts in src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py:484-540 parse_duration:

1. dcat snooze ID 999999999999d raises OverflowError (timedelta caps at 999_999_999 days; int has no max). Caught by the bare except in _cmd_workflow.py:1688, but the user-facing error is meaningless in a snooze context.

2. dcat snooze ID 9999-12-31 silently orphans an issue — filtered out of every list view (snoozed_until > now). Recoverable only via dcat unsnooze if the user remembers the ID.

3. The 'm' unit means 30 days (documented in code comment) but src/dogcat/cli/_completions.py:385-386 advertises '1 month' / '3 months' in tab completion. Drift up to 3 days for 1m (Feb non-leap), up to 2 days for 3m.

Fix: clamp amount to ~36500 days with a friendly error; cap snoozed_until at a 10y horizon (or surface year-9999 snoozes prominently in dcat doctor); rename completions to '~1 month' or use dateutil.relativedelta for true calendar months.
acceptance: Snooze duration overflow shows a clear error; far-future snoozes (>10y) are rejected or flagged by doctor; completion labels match actual semantics.

acceptance.new: Snooze duration overflow shows a clear error; far-future snoozes (>10y) are rejected or flagged by doctor; completion labels match actual semantics.

resolve_partial_id("") matches every issue ID
description:
src/dogcat/_id_resolve.py:50-54 builds matches via 'full_id.endswith(partial_id)'. For partial_id='', endswith returns True for every full id. With one matching issue in the database the resolver returns that issue.

Trigger: any CLI command whose first positional arg is an id and the script forgot to validate it. dcat close "", dcat update "", dcat delete "", dcat inbox close "", dcat comment "" add --text x, dcat dep "" add --depends-on x, dcat link "" add --related x — all silently mutate the only matching issue/proposal.

Impact: silent destructive mutation. The lone-issue case has no ambiguity to detect.

Fix: reject 'not partial_id' at the top of resolve_partial_id.
acceptance: resolve_partial_id('') returns None (or raises) without scanning the id set; tests cover empty/whitespace partial IDs across issues and proposals.

Non-dict JSONL line crashes _load with uncaught AttributeError/TypeError
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:155-186 (and src/dogcat/inbox.py:87-117) catch (orjson.JSONDecodeError, ValueError, KeyError). A line whose JSON decodes to null/42/[]/'string' returns a non-dict; the next data.get(...) raises AttributeError, which is NOT in the except. The 'tolerate corrupt last line' branch lives behind the same except, so even last-line non-dict lines bypass tolerance.

Mid-file occurrence aborts every dcat invocation that constructs storage (list, show, update, close, doctor, archive, web, TUI). Recovery requires hand-editing issues.jsonl which CLAUDE.md forbids.

Same pattern in src/dogcat/stream.py incremental parser, src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_archive.py raw-line iteration, src/dogcat/merge_driver.py.

Fix: add (AttributeError, TypeError) to the except tuples; or 'if not isinstance(data, dict): continue' after orjson.loads. _validate.parse_raw_records already does the latter — copy it.
acceptance: A non-dict JSONL line anywhere in issues.jsonl/inbox.jsonl logs a warning and is skipped; CLI commands continue to function; regression tests cover null, list, scalar at first/middle/last positions.

Mid-file malformed JSONL line bricks the CLI
description:
src/dogcat/storage.py:174-185 only forgives the literal last non-blank line via 'is_last_line'. Any other malformed line — truncated record from an editor crash, partial UTF-8 from an interrupted append, BOM at the head, future enum value — re-raises ValueError and breaks every subsequent dcat invocation. No in-tool repair; CLAUDE.md forbids hand-editing.

Same pattern in src/dogcat/inbox.py.

Fix: switch to skip-and-warn for any malformed line (matching _validate.parse_raw_records behavior); set self._needs_compaction = True so the next mutation rewrites cleanly. Add a 'dcat admin repair-jsonl' that copies bad lines into a .bad sidecar.
acceptance: A malformed mid-file JSONL line is logged as a warning and skipped; subsequent dcat calls succeed; doctor surfaces the count of bad lines; new admin subcommand exists for explicit repair.
